name: 101 Physio - Brampton
address: 2 County Court Blvd, Suite 230, Brampton, ON L6W 3W8
description:
I had such a great experience at this physio clinic. My physiotherapist, Rizia, was absolutely amazing. She was knowledgeable, patient, and really took the time to explain everything clearly. I felt genuinely cared for during every session, and she made sure I was comfortable and confident with my treatment plan.

I also want to mention how kind and welcoming the entire staff is. The secretary and everyone at the front desk are very, very kind and always greet you with a smile. It honestly makes such a difference walking into a place that feels so warm and supportive.

Highly recommend this clinic and especially Rizia!

I had a great experience at CBI Health for my physiotherapy. From the moment I walked in, all the staff were friendly, welcoming, and very helpful. They made the whole process smooth and comfortable.
A special thanks to Riza, my physiotherapist, who really took the time to understand my condition and provided treatment that best suited my needs. I truly felt cared for and supported throughout my sessions.
Highly recommend CBI Health to anyone looking for professional and compassionate physiotherapy services.

I had an amazing experience with my physiotherapist Riza Mansuri. . From the very first session, they made me feel comfortable, heard, and genuinely cared for. They took the time to properly assess my condition, explain everything clearly, and create a treatment plan that actually worked for me.

What I appreciate the most is their patience and professionalism. They never rushed appointments and always encouraged me throughout my recovery. I’ve noticed significant improvement in my pain and mobility, and I feel much more confident in my body now.

If you’re looking for someone knowledgeable, kind, and truly dedicated to their patients’ progress, I highly recommend them. I’m really grateful for all the support and guidance I received.

I have been going to physio here for several months now and the staff is very friendly and helpful.  My therapist Riza is very calm, and kind and makes sure that the exercises she gives me do not cause me more pain.  She has taken the time to do things beyond the original scope of my sessions like teach me how to use kinesiology tape in order to alleviate pain in my hip and knee.  She even used my phone to take videos of me doing the assigned exercises so I could watch them at home and replicate the movements.  This is very helpful as I was having trouble remembering all of the exercises she was showing me. This is the third CBI location in Ontario that I have visited and I am extremely pleased with the overall service.

Everyone at CBI Brampton were professional, courteous and a pleasure to deal with. My therapist, Riza, really knew her stuff. Her positive attitude really helped me get over my doubts and concerns about my recovery. I would recommend CBI to anyone who needs physiotherapy.

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - College Station
description:
Jake was incredibly helpful with rehab for my shoulder injury. He has a vast knowledge on anatomy/movement and was able to explain things to me very well. Not only did he provide me with knowledge and exercises for strengthening but he gave me a lot of tips on how to modify my workouts in the gym to prevent worsening the injury. The clinic was great and I never have to wait long to be seen, or have issues with billing. Thank you so much!

I had a severe shoulder injury from playing squash and dealt with pain and limited range of motion for over 10 months. Working with Luke Gregg at Cornerstone Physiotherapy completely changed things. He put together a clear rehab plan with effective exercises, and within about three months I fully recovered. Luke is knowledgeable, attentive, and truly cares about his patients’ progress. I’m now pain-free and back to full function. I’m extremely satisfied with the care I received and highly recommend Luke to anyone dealing with a challenging injury.

Alex was great to work with for my sciatica. He was thoughtful, knowledgeable, and genuinely cared about helping me get better. He took the time to explain what was actually causing the issue and showed me simple, practical exercises that made a real difference. I really appreciated how clear and approachable he was…. it helped me feel confident about managing it going forward. Very thankful for the support and care he provided.

Met Kyle for the first time for a back injury.  My appointment was early at 7:15am and he was ready for me upon arrival.  He completed the assessment asking many questions to ensure he understood my issue.  We worked in the gym as well as some physical manipulation which I prefer.  He checked in many times to ensure I was comfortable and not experiencing any pain.  Through his words and actions, he seems very committed to my rehabilitation.  This includes an upcoming trip and having the ability to email him any questions, should I experience any discomfort associated with my back.  Impressive!  Thank you, Kyle.  I look forward to our next session.

Alex has been a huge help with the preparation and recovery process for my shoulder surgery. He helped me build strength prior to the surgery to make the recovery process easier, and then helped me steadily make progress back to pre-surgery mobility and strength goals after the surgery was done. Would recommend!

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - Downtown Toronto
description:
After over a year of dealing with vertigo and a faint diagnosis from a family doctor, Lauren Philippi confirmed it was BPPV and in 2 visits plus some at-home exercises, my life was back to normal!

I had forgotten how nice is to look everywhere without getting dizzy.

Lauren is obviously super knowledgeable and also incredibly kind. I felt very supported through the entire process and her explanations are very detailed. Highly recommend it.

I visited Cornerstone physio at the Downtown location for pelvic floor physiotherapy. At first I was hesitant about treatment but luckily I found Rosie who guided me and actually relieved the issues I was having. Rosie is a good listener. She really listens, gives great advise on exercise and lifestyle changes, makes you feel comfortable, knows what shes doing and she truly cares about her patients prognosis.  She is also a sweet and down to earth person. Thank you Rosie Im so happy I found you :)
Bessie

I had such a great experience with Steven Khuu! He was very patient, empathetic, and knowledgeable. I came in with neck, shoulder, hip, lower back, and ankle problems, and he helped me through all of them step by step.
What I really appreciated was how he explained everything clearly and adjusted the treatment to my needs.
Now I’m pain-free and finally back to living my normal life. I’m so grateful for his help and would definitely recommend him to anyone looking for an excellent physiotherapist!
Also the front desk team was  super nice, helpful, and friendly. Highly recommend!

I was very worried about my Bell’s palsy, but Ross Cameron made me feel safe and comfortable from the first session. He is extremely patient, explained everything in detail, and showed excellent knowledge. He gave me a positive and reassuring experience throughout. I’m very thankful for his support. Highly recommend!

Laurel Brewster is a really great physio. I'm grateful for her attention to detail, thorough knowledge, and genuine care to get me back to my best.

I had a tear in my quadriceps over a year ago that I got treatment for at a different physio clinic and suffered re-injuries after two separate treatment periods. I went to another clinic to try a new physio and the same occurred. It was finally after hearing about Cornerstone through a friend that I came and saw Laurel and she helped me find the root area of injury (rectus femoris tendon) to properly heal and strengthen it. Had no issues with it since, courtesy the exercises she gave me that I consistently do now. Had a minor hamstring tear recently which is when I came back to see Laurel and that has been taken care of as well.

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - North York
description:
Cornerstone is a wonderful clinic with clean rooms, great equipment, and super knowledgeable staff. Sean has been instrumental in my recovery from a hip and back injury. He provided personalized exercises that quickly helped me strengthen my weaker muscles. He was also incredibly kind and made me feel very comfortable throughout the process. Best physio experience thus far.

I had an excellent experience with my physiotherapist Sean Gill. Incredibly professional, knowledgeable, and attentive throughout every session. From the very beginning, he took the time to thoroughly assess my condition and clearly explain the treatment plan in a way that was easy to understand.
What truly stands out is how approachable and friendly he is. He creates a comfortable environment where I feel heard and supported. No question ever feels rushed or dismissed—he makes sure I fully understand the exercises, the purpose behind them, and what to expect during recovery.
His clear explanations and step-by-step guidance have made the entire rehabilitation process much less overwhelming. Thanks to his expertise and encouragement, I’ve seen steady improvement and feel more confident in managing my recovery.
I highly recommend him to anyone looking for a skilled, professional, and genuinely caring physiotherapist.

Charlotte is an outstanding physical therapist! She is professional, knowledgeable, patient, and truly cares about her patients.

She explains everything clearly and creates the most suitable treatment and rehab plan based on your condition. She taught me many useful practices that help with my recovery process and daily life.

She consistently adjusted the approach based on how I was feeling, which made me feel heard and confident in the process, she can even remember my last symptoms and progress every time!

Thanks to her, my lower back pain has improved significantly. I’m so grateful for her help and I highly recommend her!

I’ve just completed a 10-week physiotherapy program with Caleb Kim for a knee injury, and I cannot recommend him highly enough. He is not only extremely knowledgeable and an excellent physiotherapist, but also very compassionate, approachable, and a great communicator. The exercises he prescribed were very helpful in strengthening my knee and reducing pain. He clearly and thoroughly explained how each exercise would help, which really motivated me to follow the routine religiously. In every session, Caleb took the time to answer all of my questions and address my concerns, making me feel well supported throughout the entire program. Now my knee feels normal and I feel ready to get back to being more active once the weather gets warmer.

I’d also like to thank all the staff at Cornerstone, who were the epitome of professionalism and friendliness. A 100/10 clinic.

I'm so grateful to Charlotte!  She put together a treatment plan for a labral tear injury in my right hip.  Charlotte also created a plan for a pelvic torsion on the same side.  All exercises were well explained, demonstrated, and then we practiced them.  The results have been fantastic - I'm thrilled.  The labral tear doesn't bother me any longer and the pelvic torsion relief has been a godsend. And, I'm still doing the exercises.  Thank you Charlotte!

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - Burlington
description:
I had a great experience at Cornerstone Physiotherapy for my BPPV treatment. The team ( Russell) was knowledgeable, patient, and took the time to explain everything clearly. After just a few sessions, my vertigo improved significantly. I’m very grateful for their care and highly recommend them!

Camilla is exceptional—highly knowledgeable, thoughtful, and an absolute professional. She has helped me address multiple issues, and I’ve seen significant, measurable progress under her care. What truly sets her apart is how she tailors each treatment to your specific needs while taking the time to educate you along the way. She explains complex medical concepts in a clear, approachable manner, which makes you feel informed, confident, and actively involved in your recovery. I couldn’t recommend her more highly.

I've been working with Russel for a while now and he's always able to quickly get me back on my feet. The sessions are very comprehensive and in depth, multiple tests are ran to determine exactly what muscles are affected, and he recommends great exercises to get back on track!

I’ve had several sessions with Jan, and I cannot commend him enough for his professionalism, thorough assessments, and excellent rehabilitation plans. He is personable, attentive, and truly invested in your progress. I had been struggling with chronic pain from a past injury and had almost given up hope of improvement—until I started working with Jan. He got me back on track.

What I particularly appreciate about my sessions is how he explains the science behind the issues in clear, simple language. He ensures you understand what’s going on before treatment, and that knowledge has helped me make smarter choices in my workouts. I highly recommend Jan and his expertise.

I had a wonderful experience with Daniel MacKinnon. He is extremely knowledgeable and clearly understands the technique he uses for dizziness and balance issues. From the very first moment, he made me feel comfortable, listened carefully to my concerns, and explained everything in a clear and reassuring way.
His treatment was gentle, professional, and very effective. I truly appreciated not only his expertise, but also his kindness, patience, and genuine care. I felt supported throughout the entire session and left feeling much better and confident in the process.
I highly recommend Daniel to anyone dealing with dizziness or balance problems.

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - Markham (Unionville)
description:
I’ve been seeing Josh at Cornerstone Physiotherapy for the past three weeks, and I couldn’t be more impressed. He is exceptionally knowledgeable, attentive, and professional. From the very first session, he took the time to understand my specific issues and tailored a treatment plan that really addressed my needs. His guidance and expertise have made a significant difference in my recovery, and I feel much stronger and more confident in my progress thanks to his support. I truly appreciate the care and dedication he brings to every session.

Roland is an amazing physiotherapist! He’s friendly, professional, and really knows what he’s doing. The treatment he gave me helped a lot with my neck and elbow pain. I felt the improvement after just a few sessions.

He always takes the time to explain the exercises and makes sure you’re comfortable the whole time. I really appreciate all his help. Highly recommend!

I had an excellent experience with Heather, a Cornerstone Dietitian. She was very knowledgeable, and always pleasant to work with. I learned a lot, and gathered very helpful information on diet and gastrointestinal issues. Best of all, my test results showed marked improvement. Highly recommended!

I had a very positive experience working with May Liu at Cornerstone Physiotherapy. Following a serious car accident, I came in dealing with multiple injuries, and May has been kind, patient, and thorough throughout my ongoing treatment.

She takes the time to listen carefully, explain each step of the process, and ensure I feel comfortable during every session. I truly appreciate her professionalism and supportive approach as I continue working through my recovery.

Thank you for your care and dedication.

You can tell how great a place is by how happy their staff appears. The staff at the front desk always greet everyone with a smile.  They are so kind and approachable that it’s a pleasure to interact with them. Roland, my physiotherapist doesn't rush, he takes his time asking pertinent questions and listens.  He not only does manual work but takes me to the gym to show me how to engage my muscles to strengthen them and more importantly avoid more issues down the road.  Thank you to the entire team at Cornerstone.

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - Richmond Hill
description:
I had a great experience with Travis as my physiotherapist. He did excellent work helping me recover from my back issues and played a big role in getting me back to good health. Travis was professional, knowledgeable, and took the time to understand my condition and tailor the treatment to what I needed.

Thanks to his care and guidance, my pain improved significantly and I was able to return to my normal activities. I really appreciate the effort he put into my recovery and would highly recommend him to anyone looking for a skilled and supportive physiotherapist.

Cornerstone Physio has been a game changer for our entire Family!!!
Aparna, our therapist is one of the most knowledgeable and understanding individuals we’ve ever met! She knows exactly how to diagnose and provide the correct physiotherapy care required for us individually.
She is so well versed and extremely intelligent when it comes to our health and longevity. We are so grateful for her. Keep up the amazing work!! Without you we’d be broken 😄

I was looking for pelvic floor physiotherapy in Richmond Hill and found out that Cornerstone offers it. I attended several sessions with Aparnaa, she us by far the most amazing physiotherapist I've seen. I went to other places in the past but my experience here exceeded what I expected. My assessment was very thorough and exercise assignments were clear and helped  reach my goals. Aparnaa is very helpful and motivating. She also helped me with another  emerging issue with my arm. I would recommend Cornerstone, especially for pelvic floor. The clinic is very clean and reception staff are very friendly and quick.

I worked with Travis McGill at Cornerstone from October 6 to November 17 for plantar fasciitis, and the experience was excellent from start to finish. As a working nurse, my schedule can be challenging, but Travis was always flexible with booking, which made it easy to stay on track with treatment.
I noticed significant improvement after just two sessions, which really impressed me. Travis has excellent communication skills and is very knowledgeable—he explained each technique clearly and adjusted both the sessions and the exercises to match my personal strength and ability, ensuring everything felt effective and manageable. I left treatment fully equipped with list of exercises to manage it at home settings.
The facility is clean and well-organized, and the staff are friendly and welcoming. The added convenience of direct billing to insurance made the whole process smooth and stress-free.
I would strongly recommend Travis McGill and the entire Cornerstone team to anyone seeking professional, personalized, and effective care.

I’m so grateful that I was recommended the Richmond Hill location through a friend.
I started therapy in September for a very complex injury.
My therapist did a full evaluation and designed a specialized plan for me.
While I had a set back (nothing to do with original injury)
I was able to be in contact with my Therapist through email, who was extremely helpful.
Once I was cleared to be able to return to the clinic they excepted me immediately and I’m back on track with amazing understanding patient knowledgeable kind staff.
Thank you to Travis my therapist.
The clinic is clean and well organized the front desk staff are helpful and flexible when it comes to appointments.

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - Toronto Beaches
description:
Three months ago, I experienced a sudden and severe case of vertigo. I was bedridden for 24 hrs before I could visit my doctor (I was in no condition to deal with waiting in an ER). My doctor referred me to the wonderful team at Cornerstone Physiotherapy, and I called the Beaches branch the next morning. I was fortunate enough to get an appointment with Jordan later that same morning. I was in rough shape, and Jordan was able to evaluate, test, and formulate a diagnosis. Jordan patiently explained what was happening to me with care and compassion. He adeptly answered questions from my wife and me, and over the course of the following weeks and months, assembled an evolving program of exercises that set me on a path of recovery. I’m happy to report that I have now made a full recovery, which was made possible only with Jordan’s guidance and support. I am truly grateful to have found a caring professional in Jordan to shepherd me through such a dark and turbulent time. Thank you with great sincerity, Jordan and the Cornerstone team.

I highly recommend Cornerstone Physiotherapy. Amy is an exceptional clinician who provided exemplary care from my initial assessment through to discharge. She employs a comprehensive, holistic approach to rehabilitation; rather than simply treating immediate symptoms, she focuses on long-term injury prevention and functional health. I am grateful for her expertise and dedicated support.

Really great experience at Cornerstone Physiotherapy. I've primarily worked with Jordan, but also a little bit with Riga (and jointly with both of them). They've helped me with numerous sports related injuries. My sessions were extremely helpful, the pace and timing were perfect, and all my concerns were carefully evaluated and followed-up. The at-home exercise descriptions on the portal were easy to follow. Both Jordan and Riga were highly attuned, not just to my injuries and recovery, but also designing a program that fit with my larger training and exercise routines. I highly recommend them!

I saw Riga for physiotherapy involving my bicep and inner elbow in the summer of 2025. Riga was attentive to my needs and concerns and was extremely thorough in pinpointing the issue. His guidance and feedback throughout the treatment process helped me make a full recovery. He provided an in-depth rationale for my treatment plan, kept accurate notes for each following appointment, and was flexible in tweaking my recovery goals. I highly recommend Riga and the Cornerstone Physiotherapy team - they provide professional and compassionate care.

I had a number of complicated shoulder-rotator issues that Joel clearly diagnosed approximately two months ago.  As a squash player at 76 it was important to me to get mobile and back into squash asap.  Joel worked on my issues with a clear focus implementing remedial in office interventions and specific at home stretching/strengthening exercises.  I quickly came to feel that this guy really knows what he is doing and he does.

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - Port Credit
description:
Stephanie Kishimoto is the BEST physiotherapist that i've ever had the pleasure of meeting and working with. She genuinely cares about her clients well-being and is an EXTREMELY personable professional! Every appointment I had with Steph had me walking out feeling better than when I went in, and not just physically but mentally as well. She's very knowledgeable in her expertise and genuinely has a passion for physio and helping people which is not always easy to find.

The first appointment is an assessment to get to know you and your body and the reason you've come in, Stephanie creates a plan for your care completely catered to you and what you need as well as what you would like out of physio. She's very client focussed and wants to figure out the general cause of your pain if it's unknown and then work on a plan WITH you to help alleviate and correct any issues.

She offered dry needling to me which I've never done and it released DECADES of pressure and pain that I never thought was possible to release. I'm so thankful that I met Stephanie and the things she has done for my care and the help she's provided have changed my life for the better. Although my care is ongoing and long-term, she's completely changed my perspective on my pain and has given me newfound hope that I can and will feel better. I feel more optimistic because of her and I truly appreciate everything she's done for me.

I highly recommend seeing Stephanie and would gladly recommend her to my friends and family as I trust her completely with not only my care but the care of my loved ones as well.

Thank you so much Steph!

I can’t recommend Stephanie enough. I originally went to her for a shoulder injury, and she has been absolutely fantastic throughout my recovery. Not only did she help me make real progress with my shoulder. She also took on my carpal tunnel issues and while this is an ongoing issue, has addressed them with the same level of care and expertise.

Stephanie is incredibly knowledgeable, attentive, and genuinely caring. She takes the time to listen, explains everything clearly, and makes you feel confident in both the treatment and the exercises. I’ve noticed a significant improvement and feel much better overall thanks to her support. A truly excellent physiotherapist — highly recommend!

I can’t say enough about my physiotherapist Steph. She met with me prior to my knee replacement surgery to review my goals and get to know me personally. Since my surgery she has been so encouraging. We agree to and adjust my treatment plan as I progress and she enthusiastically celebrates my milestones with me. Her knowledge of anatomy and reasons why certain things hurt like they do is impressive. Every visit there’s always a warm greeting from Roxanne on the front desk. I look forward to my visits.

I began seeing Stephanie Kishimoto for tendinitis in my elbow, and I cannot thank her enough for the care and dedication she provided. I had been in constant pain for months, but through Stephanie’s persistence and expertise, I am now pain-free for the first time in a long time.
Stephanie has a wonderful demeanor and genuinely caring personality. She takes the time to explain everything thoroughly and always sends you home with helpful exercises and activities to support your recovery. During my treatment, we also incorporated shockwave therapy, which was a complete game changer, as well as dry needling, both of which significantly contributed to my progress.
Stephanie is professional, kind, and incredibly attentive. Her bubbly personality and genuine compassion made me feel truly seen and heard throughout my treatment. I highly recommend her to anyone seeking exceptional care.

The Cornerstone Physiotherapy at Port Credit is neat and clean, and staffs are very nice. Daniel Ramires is my physiotherapist, who is very experienced, professional and responsible. He spent time to diagnose the root course of my low back pain and developed a treatment plan during the initial session. I was impressed that Dan followed up an email after each treatment. The email covered the summary of the treatment and the exercise of the program that I need to practise at home every day. I strongly recommend this place to people who are looking for professional physiotherapy.

name: Cornerstone Physiotherapy - Newmarket
description:
I cannot say enough good things about Cedric Quach at Cornerstone Physiotherapy in Newmarket. I went in suffering from vertigo neuritis and BPPV, and it was honestly such a scary and overwhelming experience. Cedric was beyond amazing from the very first appointment.

He took the time to thoroughly assess my symptoms, clearly explain what was happening, and made me feel completely reassured throughout the process. His knowledge and expertise in treating vertigo were evident right away. He was patient, compassionate, and incredibly professional.

The treatment techniques he used were effective, and I started noticing improvement quickly. Most importantly, he gave me confidence and tools to manage my symptoms, which made such a difference.

If you’re dealing with vertigo, dizziness, or BPPV, I highly recommend Cedric. I’m so grateful for his care and support!

I had a great experience working with Cedric. He’s calm, professional, and very skilled at identifying what’s actually going on rather than just treating symptoms. His explanations made sense, and the exercises he gave were realistic and effective. I would absolutely recommend him to anyone needing physiotherapy.

Cedric is an excellent, experienced physiotherapist. He is knowledgeable, professional, and very caring. He took the time to listen, explained everything clearly, and tailored the treatment to my needs. I noticed real improvements in my mobility. Highly recommend Cedric for anyone looking for quality physiotherapy care!

This is an excellent clinic with amazing and supportive staff. I worked with Cedric for over a year. He was not only friendly and lovely to work with, he was also incredibly knowledgeable and thorough in his practice. Cedric helped me resolve my various injuries and create a plan to rebuild stronger than ever before. Highly recommended clinic!

I have been working with Cedric Quach at Cornerstone Physiotherapy for various issues over the past couple of years, first at the Toronto location (Yonge & Sheppard) and now in Newmarket. Cedric is caring, compassionate, and an excellent physiotherapist. He has consistently helped me address my issues with effective, thoughtful treatment. I highly recommend him.

name: Ministry of Health
description:
A Trillium phone agent named Elizabeth shouted at me and hung up on me today. I submitted a complaint online.

She wouldn’t last a day working for my former employers and I have 2 employers that want me back as an employee when my daughter is old enough to go to school. If I shouted and hung up on somebody, I wouldn’t be invited back to work or I’d be let go.

A lot of people are capable of shouting and hanging up a phone call, not acceptable that Elizabeth did this. I asked a supervisor or leader to listen to my phone call. Let’s hope I don’t receive a phone call with another woman screaming and hanging up…. Take your meds ladies!

I've spent 1.5 hours trying to reschedule a mammogram. I've had to make 12 calls to find a clinic where I can get an appt. I found 4 months wait time. This is unacceptable & so dangerous.
HORRIBLE SERVICE.

How is it that a genuine art piece takes at least an hour to create, but my doctors' visits each take less than 5 minutes? If I had power, that's the first thing I'd start questioning.

I am blind. Parapeligic, have Parkinson  have a major seizure disorder. Have no help at home. No nursing care for bed soars. The community paramedics won t even come cause I have too many seizures but I can t get help for seizures. So I m left to slowly die at home. I spend a lot of time on the floor unconscious. I am so afraid for my son s generation, healthcare is not accessible now if your very sick, I pity next generation

Please don’t be mad with my email because many like me try to get touch The Prims eye clinic office and it is painful and no longer possible to get touch with my eye doctor.
You better advise them to change their way of making appointments and changing appointments.

name: GTA Physiotherapy
address: 3045 Southcreek Rd, Unit 16, Mississauga, ON, L4X 2X7 (inside MSM Fitness)
description:
Sylwia is truly in a league of her own. Her knowledge and professionalism are unlike anything I have experienced before. She read me like a book — discovering and addressing things that no one else had ever been able to identify or improve. I am not writing this because she needs my review. She doesn't. I am writing this because others deserve to know that this level of expertise exists. I will always be grateful for what she has done for me. If you are looking for someone who truly sees you — Sylwia is it.

I have been seeing Sylvia for treatment of chronic neck, back and shoulder pain caused by my job for several months. Sylvia is great at what she does. My pain has subsided but I continue to see Sylvia every two weeks to keep myself in top form for work. She is friendly and caring and very professional. I cannot stress enough how great she is at her profession.

Sylwia is amazing! If you need fixing she can do it. She has an amazing ability to look at you, see imbalances and fix them. She's trained in many areas that really allows her to understand the body (trainer, rmt, osteo). She's helped me with issues that the chiropractor and my physio (who is also very good) couldn't help me with. Highly recommend! I've never stuck with an RMT until her and I've been going to her for years.

Vuk is an amazing therapist! He is very professional, kind, and skilled in knowing how to address specific issues. He took time to understand my problems, then explained his approach. The experience was very personalized and relaxing and I felt amazing afterward. He even took time to explain exercises that I can do to help the problems. I highly recommend Vuk!

I have been to a few massage therapist but when you need someone who can assist with an injury you need to see Sylwia. She is amazing, you never are sore after and she know exactly what to do and coaches you to help yourself. I won’t go anywhere else.

name: Assistive Devices Program
address: 5700 Yonge St, 7th Fl, Toronto, ON M2M 4K5
description:
A moderate sense of the Canadian healthcare system is that it is in broken if I say it is not corrupted! In my country of origin, Iran, top ranked students are selected to be educated as the future doctors. Many of these doctors have immigrated to Canada and working as Uber driver or food delivery! Highly talented and educated doctors are doing other jobs in Canada just due to the irrational regulations. Now, we should spend 6 hours at emergency room at Children Hospital while our daughter's health condition is unstable with a fever of 40 degrees. Nothing just saying shame on whom have benefited from such a broken system! Change the regulations and benefit from the presence of immigrants doctors in Canada!

Government shouldn't allow insurers and drug companies to direct patients to specific pharmacies. Instead our government turns a blind eye for their own benefits and in the so called name of saving taxpayers money.  My doctor and nurse said that I have freedom of rights as a patient to choose any pharmacy that I wish to use for my prescriptions. It is not right that patients are being coerced to receive their medications from some unknown dispensary through the mail. I know of several people including myself who called the Ontario College of Pharmacists. We were all given the same sad line that basically it's not their job and we're left to fend for ourselves. If what they're saying is true, then who will protect the patients and vulnerable citizens when it really matters and when those authorities we are suppose to trust turns us away?

The Ontario health system is a joke. Utter garbage. My respect goes out to all the doctors, nurses and all other care workers who do the best they can with what they have, which is very little. Cuts have resulted in destroying a once great system. Shame.

Very very very poor service

name: National Seating & Mobility
description:
The manager is terrible, they do not know how to deal with customer's in person about product/service issues. Very dismissive and uncaring. This is not the first instance where I have been in the store and seen this type of behaviour from management or even the sales staff talking about other customers and making fun of them. I want to add that no sales staff were on the floor they always sit at their desks when I have been in, chatting amongst themselves or are on their personal devices. Terrible customer service.

Shaina B. was a wonderful help -- so patient and kind. Thanks for a great customer experience!

I recently had a stairlift installed.
Sylvie was extremely helpful and responded to my emails promptly.
Their price was much better than others.

My uncle’s wheelchair stopped working out of the blue after not being used for 3 weeks while my uncle was in hospital. He depended on his wheelchair to get him around from point A to point B after his surgery.
He called National Seating & Mobility and he spoke to Sylvie. She managed to find him an appointment within a couple of days. Alex came in a timely manner and was able to determine what the issue was with my uncle’s chair. Alex was efficient, knowledgeable and offered great customer service. As soon as Alex left,  my uncle was already out the door, back in his wheelchair and already back to his normal routine. Thank you again so much for the great service.

I have rented equipment from National previously and I am renting equipment from them now for another post surgery.  They are always so helpful, from the employees on the telephone right through to the delivery employees.  Set-up was thorough, men very friendly and knowledgeable and answered all my questions.  I would highly recommend them again and again!

name: Infinity Health Centre
address: 39 Lower Simcoe Street, Toronto, ON M5J 3A6
description:
I’ve visited this walk-in clinic twice, and overall, my experiences have been positive. During my first visit, I was impressed by how quickly I was seen and treated. The service was efficient, direct, and to the point, which I appreciated, especially when time is of the essence. The second visit was equally smooth in terms of scheduling. I was able to book a same-day appointment, which can be incredibly difficult at other clinics. This kind of accessibility makes a big difference when you need prompt medical attention. The location of the clinic is another plus, it’s easy to find and very convenient to reach, which reduces stress when you're not feeling your best.

One area where I think there's room for improvement is in the pace of the consultations. Both times I felt the physicians were a bit rushed and dove straight into diagnosis and treatment without much conversation. While efficiency is good, it would be helpful if the practitioners took a bit more time to show care and attentiveness toward patients. That said, during my second visit, the physician did ask thoughtful questions that helped uncover the issue, which I appreciated.

Overall, I recommend this clinic if you're looking for straightforward, fast medical service in a convenient location. I’m giving this 5 stars because the service was fast, efficient, and easy to access, but I do hope the physicians can slow down a bit to show more care.

Good timely appointment with good treatment. I received the care with Dr Daria Pylypiak for my postpartum issues. Especially for trigger finger, wrist pain and for pelvic muscle pain. She was patient and friendly enough to answer all my queries and gave very good guidance. I really hope i could get Dr Daria as my family doctor who is very hospitable. It was a snowy stormy day but still Dr was available and very attentive and helpful. It seems currently there isnt a vacancy but hope to get her availability in future.
Pinpoint infinity health center has been very friendly and prompt with my appointment scheduling with great understanding of my pain and my situations. I was referred for a scan and xray and i had it done on friday and next week on wednesday after the holiday end on monday had immediate appointment booking without delay.

Here's the timeline of my visit and interactions with this clinic. I had a toe injury from playing soccer:

1. I submitted a request for appointment online Monday around 1pm (walk in).
2. In 20 minutes I got a call from them asking me what time I would like to come for the appointment. I booked for 3:45 the same day.
3. I arrive a few minutes early, then see the doctor and book an X-ray at another location, and leave before 4pm.
4. I did the X-ray on Tuesday, the day after.
5. Nothing on Wednesday.
6. On Thursday morning 8am I got a call saying the doctor got my report, and I can come in same day at 9:15.
7. I got my follow up, and doctor gave me some options and mentioned they would contact their physio which came later that day, and I got a final call at 3:40pm explaining my options.

Excellent experience, this will be my go to if I need any medical help in the future.

Note: they have a $20 late fee policy if you arrive late to the appointment. I arrive 4 minutes late on Thursday but nothing was said about it. Aim to be on time.

I came to this walk-in clinic as an absolute last resort after experiencing severe symptoms for a few days. I want to be clear that the assistant doctor who initially saw me was kind, patient, and genuinely compassionate, and I appreciate him greatly.

Unfortunately, the experience went downhill once the main doctor entered the room. I was visibly unwell, in pain, congested, and struggling to breathe properly. I had been wearing a face mask when I stepped in the clinic but pulled it down because I genuinely could not breathe with it on due to how sick I am. I apologized to the assistant doctor out of kindness, who was understanding and reassured me it was okay and that he was wearing one so it's completely fine.

The main doctor Dr brodzik however showed no patience or compassion. Instead of addressing my condition with care, he made a dismissive comment telling me to “just take the mask off since there’s no point having it on your neck.” Not professional at all to say such a snickering comment to a patient who is sincerlly in pain. No reassurance, no empathy, and no medication or meaningful treatment was offered despite how unwell I was. I left feeling brushed off and unheard.

My condition worsened to the point that I had to go to the hospital afterward, which says a lot about how inadequate this visit was.

What made this experience even more upsetting was the front desk’s behavior after I left. I called the clinic to confirm the correct spelling of the doctor’s name for my review. The receptionist was hostile, rushed through the spelling, repeatedly agreed to incorrect spellings, and seemed reluctant to provide the information at all. Once I finally confirmed the correct spelling myself *Dr brodzik* she hung up on me. This was extremely unprofessional and only made the situation worse. When she could have handled it with better customer service and compliant management instead of obviously showing the values and principals this clinic runs off of.

When people come to a walk-in clinic, they are often scared, sick, and in pain. Basic kindness, patience, and professionalism should not be optional. If a doctor is having a bad day, it should not affect how patients are treated. We come here seeking help, guidance, and care — not to feel dismissed or disrespected.

I am deeply disappointed and would not recommend this clinic based on my experience. I also want to share how low and manipulating this clinic is to offer a $200.00 dollar Amazon gift card for a google review which was displayed on their front desk which only makes me question why they have to bribe patients to get a good review. Take that into your thoughts and evaluation before bringing your loved ones here.

I had physiotherapy sessions with Tom Van Houtte for a partial ACL tear and had a great experience. Each session, my progress was carefully reviewed, and exercises were adjusted based on what still needed work. He always asked about my concerns and explained clearly and scientifically why certain symptoms remained and how to improve the recovery process. Professional, patient, respectful, and very knowledgeable. Highly recommended.

name: Infinity Health Centre
address: 2441 Lakeshore Rd W, Oakville, ON L6L 5V5
description:
I have visited the walk-in clinic twice over the last few weeks as I had bronchitis and an ear infection. Both times I saw Dr. Sharman and I was pleased with his assessment and treatment. He is friendly and easy to talk to. He also tends to be quite honest, but uses humour to accompany his candour.
I am very impressed with this clinic in terms of the ease of getting seen to if I need an appointment on the same day. The personnel are also wonderful; friendly, cheerful and helpful.
I would highly recommend this clinic for anyone living in and around the Brontë Village area, particularly for the walk-in aspect as that is all I can speak to, but it is important to have access to such services especially when one is disabled or elderly, etc.

Fantastic experience – highly recommended! I brought my child in for an appointment and was truly impressed by the exceptional care we received. Roya, Yang, and Dr. Sharman were all incredibly friendly, compassionate, and knowledgeable. Yang’s upbeat and outgoing personality brightened our day from the moment we arrived, and Dr. Sharman went above and beyond by carefully reviewing past notes and X-rays to ensure the best possible care. That level of attention and concern is something I haven’t seen at other walk-in clinics. The staff here made us feel valued and cared for—thank you for such a wonderful visit!

I recently visited Dr. Phil Wessel at PinPoint for help with hip inflammation, and I’m very thankful for his expertise and care.

His calm approach, detailed explanations, and practical home exercises made a big difference. I started feeling better day by day.

Dr. Wessel’s chiropractic experience and genuine attention made the recovery process much smoother. I highly recommend him to anyone dealing with pain or mobility issues.

Thank you, Dr. Phil, for your professionalism and support throughout my recovery!

Charbel

I have only had spectacular experiences at this clinic. I've gone 3 different times and had great experience with wait times, the Drs and nurses I saw and the reception were wonderful. I am so thankful for this clinic! - Chloe Stell

Dr SHARMAN is the absolute best.  Everything I need he is always eager to help me,  the Asian nurse is always so polite & the receptionist is so kind.  I really love this place.   Thank You 🙏 - JayT Warsh

name: Physiotherapy On Wheels
address: 6295 Mississauga Road, Suite 130, Mississauga, Ontario L5N 1A5
description:
I had an amazing experience at Physiotherapy On Wheels. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted with a warm welcome. Abbey provides amazing care, from the first treatment, she has greatly helped me in my recovery journey.  Highly recommend Abbey and Physiotherapy On Wheels to anyone looking for quality care!

I have been dealing with shoulder and knee pain for some time now and came in to see Matthew, who has been amazing. It has bin a bit over a month now and still a bit of work in progress, but I am seeing a great improvement in my range of motion and pain. I had been here for previous issues and found all of their staff to be really great. Would highly recommend them.

Matthew K. is hands down one of the best physiotherapists I've ever got to deal with in my life. Ever since my accident he has been there for me in my rehabilitation in order to get my life back on track. I honestly don't know where I would be without him. Thank you so much Matthew for all your hard work and dedication towards my recovery

I had a fantastic experience with Physiotherapy on wheels  and my physiotherapist, Alan. From the first appointment, I felt welcomed and cared for. They took the time to assess my condition thoroughly, explained the treatment plan clearly, and answered all my questions with patience and professionalism.

Over the many years of attending his clinic, I noticed  improvement in my mobility and pain levels. The exercises were tailored to my needs and adjusted as I progressed. I appreciated the hands-on techniques and the practical advice for managing my symptoms at home.

The clinic Itself is clean, well-equipped, and runs on time. The staff are friendly and helpful, creating a supportive environment for recovery. I highly recommend Physiotherapy on wheels to anyone seeking effective and compassionate physiotherapy care.

Referred to by my family doctor for my recurring back issues/pains, I visited and received physio care for the first time in my life, and wow, I was in awe how fast & well my pains were mitigated by targeted treatments/exercises by Matthew, my physiotherapist and now my "hero". My golf buddies now know whom to approach with all those aches and pains from golf swings. And I can confidently encourage other aching golfers to visit Physiotherapy On Wheels as we all need to play this game life long. Lastly, facility is clean and well equipped, and staffs are pleasantly friendly and courteous.

name: Holland Bloorview Kids Rehabilitation Hospital
address: 150 Kilgour Road, Toronto Ontario Canada M4G 1R8
description:
We had the greatest therapist, tech support was amazing. But when we had issues where the school violated our child’s human rights they 100% took the schools side and did not support our child. The school needed so much more AAC training and Holland Bloorview released our son to wolves without reservation. We can never forgive them for that.

They have a process you can request with your child’s records called a lockbox. I would definitely look into that.

This place is amazing and so good for special needs kids. The staff are great.  My son is autistic goes here for his dentist visits.

Got an opportunity to visit the hospital and volunteer for a day here. Well maintained facility for kids to support them throughout their life

Thanks to my PT at Casey House for the referral to the orthotics department at Holland Bloorview.  Easy COVID screening protocols, flew through admin and onto my appointment.  Resident was extremely professional, thorough and kind.  She very diligently walked me through the entire process (pun intended) right through to booking the follow-up.  I feel like I'm in good hands here.  Beautiful facility.

We had the absolute pleasure of working with Charise MacDonald and Kristyn Lee as our seating advisors for our child, Ali—and we couldn’t be more grateful. From the very beginning, Charise and Kristyn showed a deep understanding of Ali’s unique needs, approaching everything with compassion, professionalism, and a genuine desire to help.

They took the time to listen, ask the right questions, and make thoughtful recommendations that truly prioritized Ali’s comfort, safety, and independence. Their expertise was evident at every step, and they worked seamlessly as a team, making what could have been an overwhelming process feel smooth and reassuring.

Ali lit up during the fittings and interactions—it was clear that Charise and Kristyn weren’t just doing a job, they were building trust and making a difference. Their attention to detail and ability to balance clinical knowledge with heartfelt care truly set them apart.

We feel so fortunate to have had them on this journey with us, and we wholeheartedly recommend them to any family looking for support in seating and mobility. Thank you, Charise and Kristyn, for treating Ali with such dignity and for making such a positive impact on our lives.

name: Seasons Brantford Retirement Home
description:
My mother, brother and I took a tour of this facility yesterday (our mother is considering joining a retirement community). In a word: IMPRESSIVE - from our liason Amanda, to every staff member we encountered (a special thanks to Dawne for our delicious lunch) , this retirement village was extremely inviting and welcoming! Thank you to everyone and especially to the residents who opened their units for us to view.  Thank you so much for inviting us into your home. Again, thank you Amanda for a most informative tour of your beautiful facility and for giving us lots to consider.

Our “loved one” has been at Season’s in their small secure memory care unit for a year and we are so very grateful to have found this lovely safe place for her. The staff have exceeded our expectations with their endless patience and friendly, attentive, compassionate care and that goes for EVERY staff person we have had contact with in this last year. Daily activities keep her busy and involved and staff treat her like family allowing her as much autonomy as possible while keeping her safe. We are informed immediately of any health concerns by phone. The food is good, the menu varied and the place is always clean. Any concerns have been instantly discussed, a solution presented and then it is added to her file for all staff to see. Meaghan and Christina have shared many suggestions with us to help us navigate this disease. Amanda and Rachel made the initial process seamless and continue to be friendly faces whenever we visit. Our sense of relief and lack of stress this past year is all thanks to Season’s staff being so caring!  Jerry and Ellen Horak

This review is long overdue and is as much for those that are considering moving into a retirement community as well as those that are dealing with the anxiety of having to convince a loved one to leave their home and move to a community.

I'll start by simply saying, Seasons Retirement Community in Brantford is one of the most wonderful places an individual could ever retire to.

The management and staff are always smiling, friendly and willing to strike up a conversation; they never fail to put a smile on my face.

I'm always amazed at how attentive the staff is to the needs and requests of the residents, from serving meals to cleaning, maintenance and just stopping by a residents room to say, Hi, do you need anything; quite extrordinary.

The management and medical staff are extremely knowledgable and treat the residents as if they are part of their own personal family; what an incredible attitude to have.

The surroundings are stunning, from the beautiful dining room to the halls, social areas and outside amenities, all kept in pristine condition.

There are also frequent special activities as well as those that are available weekly for the residents to enjoy.  They even have a hair salon on site for those so inclined and as most residents appear to be women, this is a definate bonus.

There isn't enough space here to describe the meals and I certainly cannot do them justice as to just how great they are.  The menu is truly amazing from week to week.  The preparation, quality and how the meals are presented on the plate and served at the table are outstanding.  I must repeat, I can not do them justice here.

My mother moved in here one year ago, at the age of 97, when she finally agreed to move out of her own home.  Within one month she was so much more aware and alert than she had been for a long time, obviously due to the interaction with others on a daily basis.  I also noticed she felt more safe than she had in her own home.  My only regret is that she couldn't have moved into Seasons years ago; she is so happy.

There is always that nagging question, is it expensive.  Well, it's a little more than most, however, I have always found you get what you pay for and Seasons certainly proves this statement out.  My Mom brought up six children and worked all of her life, not retiring until she was 92, so she is entitled to the best.

In closing, in the event I personally need to move into a retirement community, I hope Amanda will make a place for me.

Thanks so very much Seasons, Douglas Grieve

All the staff at Brantford Seasons have been absolutely fabulous !  They've been taking wonderful care of our mother and a 5 star rating is well deserved by all ! The genuine care and patience shown by all staff is heartwarming and so much appreciated !  Thank you all at Seasons Brantford !
David

The staff at Seasons are incredible to deal with. They take excellent care of my family members. Christina and Amanda are fantastic. I rest easy knowing that my family is safe and being taken care of with them there. I highly recommend Seasons independent living side.
I do not recommend Seasons Memory care side. They are not adequately trained or staffed to be dealing with people with memory care issues. Place your loved one in trained nursing care.

name: Seasons Dufferin Centre Retirement Home
description:
The front desk staff and support staff are wonderful! Specific shout out to Deanna and Rosie, they are always so kind and personable every time I come in to see my loved one.

The staff treat my loved one very well and are always willing to help her when she has any questions. They even treat her dog very well and give him lots of love when they see him in the halls.

The two pieces of feedback I have that would improve the overall experience would be 1. Add more variety to the menu for residents. My loved one is not a big eater and with the repetitiveness of the meal menus she doesn't get excited and is willing to skip meals (which she should never do) 2. When I've had to reach out to management to get answers to specific questions, I have found it takes quite a while to get the requested information and on a couple of occasions I've had to follow up to ensure it wasn't forgotten about.

Overall I love The Seasons and I'm happy that my loved one is safe, happy and getting the assistance she needs from staff, but there are a couple issues that if solved would only make this retirement community even better.

My Mother in Law resides at the Trenton facility. I am always impressed by the cheerful greeting I receive each time I visit. The staff in general are very hard working, cheerful and always willing to go out of their way to be helpful. The food selection is very generous and there are always at least two choices for each meal. The place is spotless, the management attentive to the residents needs and all financial transactions are thoroughly explained. With access to bus trips to stores and fun activities I rate this facility very high and am pleased that my Mother in law also finds it a comfortable place to enjoy her golden years. Gerry Quick

After several months of frustration it has become necessary to express my insights to the Head Office of Seasons Dufferin in  Trenton ON.
It is obvious that our lives have been totally disrupted by the ongoing renovation plans where none of the residents know what we are going to end with. The attitude seems to be ‘Sorry like it or leave’. The local management team have assured us that none of these costs will find their way into our monthly fees. That’s good. Those of us who liked the wood grain décor are screwed because it is all disappearing.
My ongoing beef is with the Kitchen. Food quality is terrible. Soup was always too salty but has lately improved. The main courses however leave much to be desired. We never see steak anymore. Chicken or pork prepared in every style imaginable is sometimes served cold. The sides like potatoes and veggies are frequently cold and/or overcooked. All in all, a far cry from the ‘Fine Dining’ expressed in your brochures. Breakfast which, to selfish me, is the most important meal has been ruined. Egg size has shrunk from large size to medium and now small. To enjoy yolk volume of 2 large eggs one needs 3 ½ medium or 6 small. What a joke. Additionally Oatmeal is often runny and yucky, juices run out. Ice cream for dessert is at a premium or not available.
It is my decision to not rely on food service from Seasons. My wife and I will order in or eat out.
Therefore I ask Seasons owners to reduce our monthly fees by the equivalent of 2 people having 3 meals a day for 30 days at $15 a meal for a total of $2700.

Tour hostess was excellent.  We are not ready just yet for the type of 'independent living' Seasons offers, but preparing ourselves for the day (sooner or later) when we will have need.  Seasons is close in location to our family.

Staff turnover is very high which indicates poor management and treatment of staff. If my Mom didn't go to the diningroom, they did not provide a meal until we intervened. Then they left the meal out of reach. They had no nursing on duty only unregulated care providers who passed out pills but new nothing about pharmacology.  My Mom paid over $4900 per month. We finally got her out of there and she is thriving. Don't be fooled by the window dressing. Seasons is a dump!

name: Seasons Stoney Creek Retirement Home
description:
We were very impressed with the facilities at the Stoney Creek location.  Our visit started with Amber taking us on a tour of the various studios, and amenities. She explained and outlined the various additional expenses, spent time to explain and answer our concerns.  This location is very clean, bright, full of friendly people ( residents and employees) and most of all is made to feel like a home away from home.

My mother has been a resident of Seasons Stoney Creek for 9 years (8yrs in Independent Living and 1 year in Memory Care).  Our dealings with Seasons Management and Staff has been nothing but a positive experience.  When a parent is moved to memory care many anxieties beset children of the parent with respect to increased care requirements for that parent.  Seasons Memory care staff has excelled in minimizing these anxieties.  Any and all questions or concerns are dealt with in a timely fashion.  The front line care personnel are actively engaged with my mother and all the residents, they are in essence now part of her family. Activities, events calendars and outings are easily to review through the Loops communication portal at any time.  There is sufficient staff on hand to ensure procedures and protocols are kept up to date and us well informed.  I highly recommend Seasons Retirement Residence as an ideal home for your loved one.

The tour with my mom was great.  My mom was impressed with the building, daily activities and her interactions with  the residents she met.  I appreciated that Amber toured my mom,  talking with her and not at her.  Answering all her questions.  Overall a great experience.

Really appreciate the staff here. My mom has been here for 2 years and she loves the servers and caretakers. She frequently mentions someone named Natasha and Vivian. So thank you to those girls for your work and all the other workers. You truly make her day brighter whenever you spend that extra moment with our loved ones.

Amber was wonderful.  I felt as though we were VIPs.  She was very knowledgable and provided us with more than enough information to make a proper decision for our mother.  Amber also makes sure that she answers her emails follows up with emails and make sure that we have had all our questions answered.

name: Rebalance Sports Medicine - Yonge & Adelaide
address: 110 Yonge Street Suite #905, Toronto, ON M5C 1T4
description:
I have  consistently attended physio appointments since October 2024 to restore and enhance my ROM (Range of Motion) and functionality due to post OP surgery for a fractured Humerus. My physiotherapist Laurie has been very supportive and dedicated to helping me reach my physio goals.From the initial assessment, Laurie, tailored a detailed and specific plan of care that met my needs.

I very much appreciate her receptive, and considerate manner when answering my questions, concerns, and suggestions when revisiting my plan of care. In addition, her instructions and follow-up pertaining to at home exercises are clear, concise, and motivate you to continue to keep going. Laurie is an outstanding physiotherapist!

Furthermore, the reception/admin staff have been nothing short of excellent! They are very patient and always willing to help and accommodate with scheduling appointments!

Rebalance is a great place to go to get better and be better!
Honestly, you will not be disappointed at the care you receive and the multitude of services they provide for your convenience, each and everyday under one roof.It is an amazing clinic!

Tara Lipitz

This is a 5 Star review to thank Mackenzie Rhodes, the physio from Physiotherapy & Chiropractic Downtown Toronto. Over an 8 to 12 weeks period, Mackenzie helped me to fully resolve shoulder pain that was limiting my movement and causing chronic discomfort. I'm 3 months fully recovered and so grateful to Mackenzie. Thank you!

The Rebalance clinic is well organized. Everything is digital (payment, forms, rescheduling). Very efficient and easy to get to location.

Helen Suen - Rebalance Sports
Physio for post full knee replacement surgery
I'm in my 7th week of recovery and ahead of schedule.  I found Helen online as well as another physio at a different business. I had several appointments with each before choosing Helen. While the other physio was good Helen stands out for her superior depth of knowledge, hands on style and personal charm. I look forward to my appointments and enjoy the healing process with her. Don't delay and make your appointment with Helen today and start feeling better.

Mackenzie helped me so much with my knee recovery. He’s very attentive and truly cares about his patients. I felt supported throughout the entire process, and the improvement in my knee made a huge difference. I highly recommend him!!!!

I have had a great experience with Mackenzie at Rebalance. He was great at diagnosing my back issue and providing a care plan that got me feeling better than ever before. The rest of the staff at the clinic were also very friendly and accommodating whenever I needed to move an appointment or change my insurance. Definitely the best place I've been for physio is Toronto.

name: Rebalance Sports Medicine - University & King
address: 155 University Avenue Suite #303, Toronto, ON M5H 3B7
description:
I've been here a few times already now to get a thorough examination of the issues I was having, and then a post-MRI review to then be given very sound advice on what I should do next. Since then, I've been receiving a curated physio treatment that has already done wonders for me, especially the follow up exercises I need to do on the side to strengthen the muscles around my injury. Everyone that works there is top-notch and I'm glad it's close to my work so I can literally book whatever time is convenient for me, yet effective. Cheers!

heather is an amazing physiotherapist for hypermobility. she is very kind, knowledgeable and knows how to move your joints around without dislocating them. excellent person to have on your care team if you sublux your joints a lot, she will put them back in gently and effectively! receptionists are also very kind and helpful

My experience with Cindy and Christopher was exceptional.  Christopher took the time to understand my issues and was able to give a solution to assist with my recovery.  Cindy's knowledge of Acupuncture was very much appreciated and her patience was exactly what I needed to feel comfortable for my first visit to the facility.  Thank you.

This was my first session at Rebalance, I met with Sasha (RMT) for back, hip and foot pain. I have struggled to find a RMT I really want to go back and see, and I would for sure go back and see Sasha. She was really communicative throughout the massage, explaining what to expect, she was doing, checking in about how I was doing. I felt comfortable the entire time and feel confident that if I needed to stop, make adjustments that it would be well received and she would work with my comfort level. Solid recommendation from my friend!

My first session for TMJ symptoms with Desiree exceeded all my expectations! Desiree is an extremely knowledgeable professional and took care in documenting my issues/ concerns/ questions and symptoms. After the consultation, Desiree tailored the treatment to my pain, concerns and symptoms. The pain and tension was relieved immediately after my first treatment and I left the clinic feeling like a new person. Already booked my next session! I highly recommend the clinic and Desiree for anyone experiencing TMJ symptoms or issues. Thank you , Desiree!

name: Bridgepoint (Sinai Health System)
description:
I give the hospital 5-star because of my personal experience. Comparing with other facilities, Bridgepoint is the right place to have your rehab there. For instance, the newer building design can offer very nice window view. There is enough room to practice or walk around. I am extremely happy with the professional services and supports from the therapists, nurses and doctors. Dr Ainsley is one of the best rehab medical team members there.

I agree that no one is perfect. I am still awaiting the staff to complete my insurance claim. But, the service level of medical care or rehab is much superior than many other places.

Canadian should be first and our governments should STOP sending funds to foreign countries and offer sufficient funds to our public hospitals. the hospital should receive funding and arrange more programs for our Canadians.

I was hospitalized for three months and the staff were great. Dr Audrey Yap is amazing. She is very attentive and knowledgeable. Dr Seema was great as well. Easy to talk to and empathetic to your emotions and how you are dealing with things. The compassion these two demonstrated was above and beyond.

(This review is about The Don Jail)

Came here for Doors Open and was impressed. The tour was informative and the interior is gorgeous. One of the best locations of Doors Open.

Bridgepoint discharged me when I was significantly frail and still had inflammation on my brain. I desperately needed the ant inflammatory medication, steroids and IVIG. they did not provide me with a walker or a shower, chair or a grab mars. The staff were more nice than the nurses as aid mics, which were very rude. I didn't think it was professional for my physiotherapist.To keep talking about my dad when I told him I didn't have a good relationship with. He kept bringing it up every session. Also, didn't like how we would bring up my child.  And he asked me if I smoke weed around my child. Despicable. The social worker was very absent and only came through once with a list of private physiotherapistss around where I live. Please then be felt useless.I have a PgD and we just read sentences, and I have to fill out a maze. How t felt useless as well. She missed a lot of classes. PT felt useless as well. I only had one class per week. If I am like how the physiotherapist kept saying they would walk normally next week when I was so frail. The cap pushing a heavy rollater on me. Is sugar showed me a lightweight collaboration. I was ready for that. We keep saying there's still a walking aids. But there's a cane and low weight rollater that I can use.

I'm at Toronto Rehab. I have PT classes 5 days a week, including Saturdays.

I also go for walks outside with my behavioral therapist and I see a rehab specialist as well.

Speech Therapy at Toronto Rehab is great. They received excellent assessment.

Now i'm healthy and ready to move into a two bedroom condo with my son

Toronto Rehab is the complete opposite. Staff are nice, and they are providing me with a walker and shower chair and possibly grab bars for when I go home this weekend. I feel fit, and I can walk and talk normally, and I am working out my gluts abs and arms. I'm also strengthening my hands with putty.

1 star sounds right.

Hope this helps.

Z

I have no clue what the inside of this fabulously located hospital was like...i was too busy on the outside lounging around by their blue poles on that awesome front lawn... I got totallly hammmered and a bit high... fell asleep between those posts waking up fresh and ready to start my lower don river adventures.. The robot and its dog? was prettty awesome! End of september... Saw some kids rollllerblading by... it was prettty lit!

Then I walked down the street and grabbed some a&w open 24h and gots hydro. was pretty saweeet tooo. with a library riverdale jus outside of. would visit again sometime sooon.... maybe even check out the inside 4th-7th floors...

name: Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation at Ontario Medical Center
description:
Disappointing Experience with Kaiser Security Department

As someone seeking care for my mother in law during a time of illness, I expected compassion and professionalism—not inconsistency and profiling. Unfortunately, my recent experience with Kaiser’s security team left me feeling singled out and discouraged.

While entering the facility, I noticed that only certain individuals—myself included—were asked if we had weapons on our person. This wasn’t a standard screening applied to all patients or visitors. It felt arbitrary, and frankly, discriminatory. In a moment when I was already worried about my mother in law as she was vulnerable and unwell, being approached in that manner added unnecessary stress and made me question the integrity of the process.

Security protocols should be consistent, respectful, and rooted in safety—not bias. When enforcement feels selective, it undermines trust and dignity. We came for healing, not to be profiled.

Kaiser Security must do better. Patients and visitors deserve to feel safe, not scrutinized. Especially when our bodies are already fighting enough and if done for one it should be done for all.

Unconscious bias training would be great and hopefully considered.

Worst experience of my life! Very dirty from waiting room to the restrooms. Girls in the front desk nonstop talking using bad language and hanging out like they are at a kick back. Monika that drew blood from me was laughing about something she seen on her phone, while im in so much pain and agony. Monika is NOT a vein whisper! She was moving that needle everywhere while inside my arm, and I have a big bruise. When I finally reached someone that cared is when I finally got to see a physician after waiting all night. The nurse that gave me my pain through IV was an angel! IF you choose to work with ppl and in the medical field make sure you have EMPATHY, INTEGRITY, AND PATIENCE. You must care about Humans at their most volunerable times. I understand that emergency requires waiting and sometimes its a long wait, but it makes it worse when you encounter unprofessionalism and a unkept area that our family's have to wait in.

5-Star Review for Phlebotomist Mariah at Kaiser Permanente Ontario

I had the most wonderful blood draw experience today! Mariah was incredibly professional, kind, and genuinely caring; you could tell she truly cares about her patients’ comfort and health. She explained everything clearly, had such a steady hand, and made the whole process completely stress-free.

Mariah is knowledgeable, gentle, and has the warmest, most thoughtful approach to patient wellness. Hands down one of the best phlebotomy experiences I’ve ever had! Thank you, Mariah, for making something that’s usually nerve-wracking feel so easy and pleasant. KP Ontario is lucky to have you.

Highly recommend requesting Mariah if you’re at the Ontario location.

Dr. Rassman is such an excellent surgeon in every way. He exudes professionalism, knowledge in his field, and gives all his patience and understanding to his patients, you can't help but only like him and trust him to be in good hands. He also has the best assistants, anesthesiologist, and the most helpful and friendliest nurses that assist him. My overall experience was excellent just as I remembered from the last shoulder surgery, I had on my other side in 2018 or 2019. Both of my daughters are RNs from City of Hope in oncology and said "he is one of the best they've met." Kaiser is blessed to have such a fine orthopedic surgeon and Dr. Rassman. I am so blessed and grateful to have him as my orthopedic surgeon.

I came to Kaiser Ontario for a regular OBGYN appointment for my second pregnancy, but due to my high blood pressure I was sent to labor & delivery for monitoring once again.  I found out the amniotic fluid was below 2 so they had to have the c-section same day.  This is our second baby and we predicted this would've happen again.  I am glad that we were able to deliver the baby at this location due to our excellent experience from our first child.

The team was extremely helpful that made me feel extremely comfortable during my stay there.  From the staff that sees me for my regular OBGYN appts to the staff that prep me ready for the c-section, the anesthesiologist whom is Dr. Neeraja Bondugula Reddy, Dr. Monika Renee Cluney whom was in charged of the c section, and the entire team that assisted with performing the operation helped us get through another successful operation experience.

Once in the PACU room and Postpartum room, the entire nursing team that we met were very professional, attentive, sweet, caring, and provided a great deal of assistance and reassurance.  We would like to recognize the amazing care that me & baby received from Charity, Jo Ann, and Tina for the 2 days.  It was another great experience as well as being smooth too.  I made sure to take the time to be able to write a recognition form for them because I know it's easily complain about the bad experience when we should also take the time to acknowledge the people that have given you the positive experience.  The room was spacious and comfortable to stay in as well. The bench my husband slept on was able to pull out.  I thought that was cool because when we had the 1st baby they didn't have that.

Thank you all so much on all of your hard work and dedication in caring for me and my baby.

While in the hospital, we were able to take advantage of the room services.  That was fun to do once again.  The meal was only for the mom and if your spouse wants to order it was going to cost $12.  The meals they provide actually tasted delicious.  Also, we got to enjoy the Celebratory Breakfast before being discharged.

We highly recommend this location when you want to give birth to your child.  The attentiveness and care really helped my recovery to be quick.

name: Toronto Physiotherapy (Danforth & Chester)
description:
I was referred to Lindsay Davey and the clinic by Sunnybrook Hospital for cancer rehab and it's been a great experience.  I have learned a lot. Lindsay has so much experience with cancer patients and draws on that knowledge, provided me with a personalized exercise routine.  She really listens to my concerns and is very patient in answering all of my questions.  Her energy is contagious!  As an added bonus the clinic is in the east end and accessible by transit.

I’ve had shoulder pain for years and started seeing Paul recently. After just two sessions, my pain is already about 90% gone, which honestly surprised me. He’s very knowledgeable, listens well, and really knows what he’s doing. The sessions felt focused and effective. I’m super happy with the treatment nd hoping that it will stay like this. Also he does dry needling which I think helps a lot

Lindsay, my physiotherapist, has provided care after my radiation therapy. She has done a great job in providing help for which I was in desperate need. Her ability to accurately and quickly diagnose my "cording" issue has provided much needed relief.
My massage therapist, Fahimeh, worked hard to address my "cording" issue.
The administrative staff at both locations have made me feel welcomed, and were flexible in arranging schedules that worked for both involved.
My experiences at Toronto Physiotherapy have made me feel that I am the luckiest person who was in search of help.
I appreciate the help I received from the Toronto Physiotherapy Clinics and highly recommend them to anyone who needs help after radiation therapy.

I saw Maddy Reinders for a very aggressive sciatica pain flare up. She was excellent and thorough and create a clean plan to get me pain free. She also gave me exercises that I could do at home to help with my progress. She also gave me very informative feedback on my form for many lower body strength training movements which contributed to my sciatica pain due to poor form and a weak core. I would definitely see Maddy again for any issues and recommend her to friends.
