I had an outstanding experience at this clinic to rehab from ankle surgery. The clinic and staff are great – in particular, my physiotherapist, Maddy Reinders, is exceptional. She was thorough, knowledgeable, and developed a clear treatment and exercise plan to meet my personal recovery goals, which I appreciated. When there were hiccups along the way, she responded by modifying the plan to quickly get me back on track. Maddy is super personable and made my appointments a joy to attend. I’m now fully healed and motivated to be even more active than I was pre-surgery thanks to Maddy. She is absolutely a 10/10 – best physiotherapist ever!

name: Toronto Physiotherapy (Yonge & St. Clair)
address: 1246 Yonge Street, Suite 306, Toronto, ON, M4T1W5
description:
I am so very grateful for Lindsay Davey - an incredibly knowledgeable and caring professional, and an overall stellar human being. Lindsay’s expertise and warm approach have helped me manage lymphedema and scar tissue from several surgeries.  She listens attentively, shares suggestions and also teaches techniques to use at home to further recovery. After each appointment with Lindsay, I feel energized and uplifted, and I cannot thank her enough for her crucial role in helping me heal. I would never wish to have the health issues I have had, but finding Lindsay Davey has most definitely been the silver lining. The clinic itself is modern and bright, well organized, making appointments is easy and the front desk staff are friendly and responsive - I highly recommend Toronto Physiotherapy!

After suffering a lower back injury that left my entire right leg—from hip to toes—completely numb, I was deeply concerned and convinced I was experiencing severe sciatica. Desperate for relief, I searched for physiotherapists who specialized in sciatica and that’s how I found Elias Peixoto.

From my very first session, I was struck by Elias’s depth of knowledge, professionalism, and genuine care. He quickly assessed my situation and developed a personalized treatment plan that included targeted acupuncture, nerve flossing stretches, and muscle rehabilitation exercises. Elias explained everything clearly and made sure I was comfortable and confident with each step of the process.

Thanks to his expertise and commitment, I experienced remarkable progress in a short period of time. Within just five weeks, my numbness had disappeared and my back pain had subsided—a truly incredible result after such a scary injury. Elias didn’t just focus on the immediate symptoms; he also equipped me with a long-term exercise routine to prevent future issues and maintain my recovery.

I am genuinely grateful for Elias’s thoughtful and holistic approach to physiotherapy. His dedication went beyond just treating my symptoms—he empowered me with the knowledge and tools to take charge of my own healing and wellbeing. If you are dealing with sciatica or any other challenging injury, I highly, highly recommend Elias Peixoto. You will be in the very best of hands.

This year I began treatment with Elias for my occipital neuralgia, and I'm very grateful for everything he’s done for me. His deep knowledge, experience, and genuine compassion made me feel understood and supported from the very first session.

The treatments and exercises he gave me have helped me regain mobility in my neck. He has also helped me with pain in my jaw, back, and hand, always with patience, care, and incredible attention to detail. He never forgets anything about my medical history, and every appointment feels like a true continuation of my healing journey.

Elias has also helped me understand that stress and anxiety can worsen the pain so he has adjusted the treatment accordingly.

Thank you, Elias, for your dedication, kindness!

I absolutely LOVE Lindsay Davey at Toronto Physio, she is amazing! She has helped me in so many ways. She is so great at thinking outside the box and will use a variety of different treatments to combat my stubborn ailments. She is unbelievably kind, she really listens to and validates how you are feeling. I can't recommend her enough.

I thought a February calf strain in the middle of marathon training was going to sideline me for a while, but Elias had other plans for me. He was able to quickly diagnose the injury, conduct some treatment in the clinic, and then prescribe me exercises to do at home. I managed to get back to full speed in just over a week thanks to Elias. In addition to knowing what he’s doing, he’s also just a great guy to talk to while you’re getting treated. I appreciate his sports and personal training background as it gives him a good basis to understand what I go through as a runner. I didn’t see him for a few months, but then returned post-marathon with a hamstring issue, then rotator cuff, and then elbow. And as with the calf, he’s been able to get me sorted out on all fronts.

I’ve also been getting some serious massages from Mona at the clinic. I’m hesitant to say too much because she’s already so popular it can be difficult to find time with her some weeks. What I will say is that she is by far the best masseuse I’ve ever had. Like it’s not even close. I can go into the clinic barely able to walk, and then I leave practically doing cartwheels.

To round out the experience, I’d be remiss if I didn’t mention the front desk staff, and specifically Megan. She’s always so polite and professional to deal with and I look forward to her greeting me with a smile when I attend the clinic.

name: Triangle Physiotherapy
address: 980 King St W, Toronto, ON M6K 3N2
description:
I highly recommend Triangle Physiotherapy at 980 King St W. My sincerest gratitude and appreciation go to the combined Triangle team of physiotherapist Dimpee Shah and RMT Bree Dileo. Two years ago, I was experiencing weakness, cramps and pains in my legs, making it difficult to walk, sit down comfortably or navigate stairs. After an unsuccessful 6 months at another clinic, it was a relief to come to Triangle Physiotherapy to have Dimpee explain the root of the problem as a lack of extension in my ITB or iliotibial band – who knew! Rather than just giving me a list of exercises to do, she gave clear explanations of the intended impact of each treatment: understanding boosts motivation! Bree’s massage work complemented the process by helping to increase flexibility in my leg muscles. What I most appreciate about Triangle Physiotherapy is the focus on practical results – together we set a goal, such as just being able to go up and down the subway stairs without pain, and then we work together on reaching that target and then going beyond it. Excellent work! Highly recommend.

I had a great experience working with Niharika. I came in with severe back pain and felt relief immediately. I was fully healed after a few sessions. She taught me quite a lot as about how to prevent this in the future. Highly recommended!

I was seeing Karan for a shoulder issue I had for a few months ago that wasn't getting better. I've made incredible improvements ever since I started working with him. He was thorough in explaining what was happening in our initial appointment and provided me with many strategies to implement outside of our sessions. He's a great physio, I would highly recommend him to anyone!

Karan has done a fantastic job helping me recover through a back injury. He is knowledgeable, highly skilled, and professional. Every session has been a great experience.

I had a running assessment with Julian and he was absolutely amazing! I received great feedback, and we worked through practical exercises, targeted stretching, and even acupuncture to improve my technique. Highly recommend Julian for any runner looking to level up their performance!

name: Foundation Physiotherapy & Wellness
address: 383 Front St W, Toronto, ON M5V 3S8
description:
I am having an excellent experience at Foundation CityPlace. Jason Ngai is incredibly professional, knowledgeable, and easy-going. He made me feel comfortable right from day one, even though I was nervous as it was my first time ever going to physiotherapy. He followed up after my appointment with a clear and detailed treatment plan sent by email, complete with photos, which I found extremely helpful.

Booking appointments is seamless thanks to the very user-friendly online portal. The front desk experience was just as great. Annabelle is warm and polite. She took the time patiently answered all of my insurance questions and on my first day told me about the complimentary beverages available during my appointment.

I truly felt well cared for and would highly recommend Jason and this clinic to anyone looking for a positive and professional physiotherapy experience.

I went to Foundation Physiotherapy for persistent pain in my right shoulder, and I couldn’t be more impressed with the experience. Tina Homayouni took the time to thoroughly assess the issue and explained everything in clear, detailed terms so I fully understood what was going on. She provided a targeted treatment plan and practical advice that immediately made a difference. Would 100% recommend

I had an excellent experience at this physiotherapy clinic. I originally came in for chiropractic treatment related to an SI joint issue and ongoing back pain, and the care I received exceeded my expectations. The practitioner took the time to thoroughly assess my condition, clearly explain what was going on, and tailor the treatment accordingly.

In addition to hands-on treatment, the strength and conditioning sessions were extremely valuable. The exercises were well-designed, practical, and directly targeted my problem areas, which made a noticeable difference in my mobility and pain levels.

The team is knowledgeable, professional, and genuinely invested in long-term results rather than quick fixes. I felt supported throughout the entire process and saw real improvement. I would highly recommend this clinic to anyone dealing with back or joint issues or looking to build strength safely and effectively.

I'm a former athlete, now in my mid 30s, and I'm still quite active (running, callisthenics, yoga, traditional sports, etc.). Tina (physio, including concussion), Niko (deep tissue massage) and Alisha (massage) are my go-to care team.  I rarely leave google reviews but wanted folks to know how exceptional, efficient, and knowledgeable they are. I would follow these clinicians anywhere! Thanks team.

I saw Jared Bruce for knee issues that left me unable to walk properly. He is extremely knowledgeable, and throughly explains the issue and recovery plan. He is sincere and follow up on his patients. And above all, he is a very pleasant person that made my visit hassle free. I cannot recommend him enough. Thank you Jared.

name: Athletic Edge Sports Medicine and Physiotherapy Toronto
address: 121 King St W Suite 1100, Toronto, ON M5H 3T9
description:
Knowledgeable, practical and helpful staff. The administrator is friendly and helpful as well. They have a vast repertoire of staff - a doctor, physio, massage therapist etc so you can have a wide team to help with your sport injury recovery.

Out of all the clinics I've been to, this is by far been the best experience. They have options that many clinics don't have and the people are great, they generally care. Reception is on top of their game, and the therapists are willing to give extra support. Props to Dr. Lum as well.

Words cannot accurately express how helpful and kind this clinic is.  Knowing that I have access to the doctors and staff at this facility gives me peace of mind that regardless of what future injuries come my way, I am in the best possible hands to deal with them.  I have referred multiple friends and colleagues to this facility, and each one of them has had the 5 star quality treatment that I have experienced

Thank you Dr. Lum and all the staff at Athletic Edge for all that you do.

I wanted to express my appreciation for Kelsey and Tuan's excellent service and care.

From the initial contact to the treatment sessions, Tuan's expertise and compassion during the treatment sessions were truly remarkable, leaving me feeling both physically and emotionally cared for.

Kelsey's follow-up was prompt and considerate, ensuring I felt supported every step of the way.

I am very happy with the results so far.  Dr. Lum diagnosed my issue quickly and set me up with a great Chiropractor (Samantha Duncan) who, after 3 sessions, almost entirely cleared up my issue which had been ongoing for over a year.  I am very happy with the results so far and would highly recommend them.

name: Myodetox
description:
I’ve had an excellent experience working with James Ma. He helped me overcome ongoing arm, shoulder, and neck pain, and the improvement has been huge. James is incredibly professional, thorough, and extremely knowledgeable. He takes the time to fully understand what’s going on, explains everything clearly, and creates a treatment plan that actually works. Every session felt thoughtful and personalized, and I always felt confident I was in great hands. Thanks to James, I’m feeling stronger, pain-free, and moving better than I have in a long time. I would highly recommend him to anyone looking for an excellent physiotherapist.

I want to thank James Ma for all his help! After just a few weeks, I felt so much better and my pain was gone. He’s really dedicated, always gives great advice, and personalizes my training based on my needs. James is super caring, always listens, and makes every session comfortable. I’d highly recommend him to anyone looking for a great physiotherapist! Thank your very much James Ma

Pulled/ tore a muscle while atretching and ended up with skin numbness on one half of  body all below the diaphragm. Toes were ticklish and very sensitive to touch and cold. This later turned into pain in thigh and side calf muscle.

Savrina Goldenberg , the therapist at Myo Yorkville - Avenue and was assigned to me. In three sessions she identified confirmed treated my numbness and pain issues through soft tissue massage, exercises( progressively modified). In a session she massaged one spot out of pain.  Numbness went down 85% and pain by 60%. She gave me exercises to do at home after training in me in person and sending videos.
I had a total of 5 sessions and Savrina.G would check if I was doing them properly during revisits. I am almost relieved of all numbness and pain is all gone below waist. Back is 85% recovered.

Savrina is excellent, listens fulyl, checks in with a senior if needed and follows up with utmost care and professionalism. Much grateful to her. Recommend 100%.

I’ve had a great experience at this location and James Ma has really helped me with multiple issues I’ve been experiencing. He has taken the time to create really well thought out plans for my recovery and his approach has been gentle yet detailed, knowledgeable and also easy for me to follow.  Very appreciative for all the help so far and highly recommend James!!

Would highly recommend Jordan Aslanidis  if you are going to this location.

I have been seeing Jordan for about 3 months and he has helped me improve my mobility and general pain in my knee tremendously.  He has provided some great stretches that really have been helping with improving my pain areas.

Great guy and very knowledgeable - would definitely recommend!

name: Alleviate Physiotherapy
description:
Amazing experience at this clinic!
I’ve had a really great experience here. My physiotherapist, Prachi, is extremely friendly and very knowledgeable. The first thing she did was perform proper tests to accurately identify the exact problem with my knee. After that, she clearly explained a detailed rehab plan to help me strengthen my knee and get back to playing sports safely.
I’m genuinely enjoying my rehabilitation journey at this clinic and feel confident about my recovery. Highly recommended!

So I have been coming here for quite a while now. My experience has been pleasant from start to current.. The billing is easy effortless its a quiet atmosphere calm you go get your treatment and leave. Pooja has been my therapist for a while now and she is amazing. She has good exercises for you to do. She is able to give you a soft tissue , deep tissue massage and she has the best elbows. She was able to find my knots in my back and work some out. Also when I told her where was bothering me she was able to target those spots without any complaints. I left feeling like a million bucks.. She is also very professional. I feel comftorable with my therapist and definitely am going back. When I go to poja I know she'll take care of me and I know what to expect each time. Im sure the other staff are just as good but I stick to poja cause I am most comftorable

Pooja was very professional and walked me through what we would be doing from the first visit through to the end of our sessions.
She is a very knowledgeable person regarding her craft and she is very genuine as a person.
I have been to other therapist and I must say Pooja as really helped me recover from hip surgery  step by step and a lot quicker then my 1st hip surgery.  I would highly recommend her to anyone.

I had a severe neck spasm and within just two days of treatment, Pooja completely relieved the pain. Her approach was highly professional, attentive and effective. She explained the process clearly , made me feel comfortable and ensured quick recovery. I truly appreciate her expertise and dedication, highly recommends her to anyone in need of physiotherapy care.

I always go to alleviate whenever i need help with a physiotherapist or chiropractor. This time Prachi helped me a lot with my neck pain. She was gentle and knew what all points to press to relieve my pain. I will definitely come back to seek help from the staff at alleviate. They are professional and helpful.

name: Trilogy Physiotherapy
address: 366 Adelaide St E #101, Toronto, ON M5A 3X9
description:
As a physiotherapy first timer and having no idea how to even find the right place for my injury, trilogy physio was one of the few places i called and for a first impression it made me feel very comfortable. Kate made me feel very safe over the phone that they would treat me well over there! I went in for a first session and my physiotherapist Jane has been the most encouraging and understanding throughout this whole new process for me. If you are a first timer to physiotherapy in general this is an amazing place to go to! Im excited to go everytime i have a session :)

Great place for all physical needs. Had several physiotherapy sessions with Neha, very professional, I feel like I have a new body right now. Place is well located for downtown and have cosy decor. Excellent service every time.

As a fitness professional, it's great to have a reliable physiotherapist in your circle.

Heather has been a pivotal person to have in my network. I always refer my clients to her if they need additional support for postural assessment, injuries, or anything that may fall out of my scope.

I always hear nothing but positive feedback from my clients' experiences with Heather.

I've also put my trust in her for my own recovery so I've experienced her skill and creative approach to physical problems first hand. I highly recommend Heather and the team for those looking to address pain, recover from an injury and improve how you move. Thanks Heather!

I have been receiving physiotherapy from Neha at Trilogy for the last month. She is attentive and takes the time to listen and understand your issue before working on a treatment plan. I've seen significant improvement in my injury Through my work with Neha and would happily recommend her services to others looking for physiotherapy.

After my persistent lower back pain did not get better after going to two clinics, I gave Trilogy a shot and Jane was the only physiotherapist that was able to get to the root of the pain by identifying the source and helping to manage the pain through targeted exercises. I can't believe my pain actually improved after years of suffering!

name: Michael Garron Hospital
description:
Michael Garron Hospital is a well-regarded community teaching hospital in East Toronto, Ontario, that often gets positive recognition for its role in providing comprehensive, patient-centred care to a large and diverse local population.

People and organizations who speak about the hospital often highlight that:

It’s been serving the East Toronto community for nearly a century, caring for hundreds of thousands of residents with a full range of services — from emergency, maternal and newborn care to pediatric, surgery, mental health, and community programs.

The Stavro Emergency Department is busy and innovative, with features like an E-Booking portal for minor injuries and a child-friendly emergency zone that makes visits less stressful for families.

It’s recognized for high standards of care and safety, with Accreditation Canada awarding it exemplary standing, meaning it meets very high quality benchmarks.

The hospital is affiliated with the University of Toronto Faculty of Medicine and other institutions, which supports a teaching environment that keeps practices current and rooted in research.

The hospital has received community support and awards; for example, it’s been named one of the Greater Toronto’s Top Employers for multiple years — an indication of good workplace culture and how it supports staff and services.

Visitors and patients often share that MGH is compassionate and professional, especially in special areas like child care and maternity. While personal experiences vary just like at any major hospital, many families report that staff are caring and that children and newborns get thoughtful attention.

Overall, Michael Garron Hospital is seen as a trusted medical cornerstone for East Toronto — strong in clinical services, supportive of diverse patient needs, and continually evolving to meet community growth.

Hi
Today, I have visited Michael Garron Hospital at 5:30 pm, following the yellow arrow & reached to RAZ.
All systems were good, less waiting time but the quality of nurses & ER doc was not satisfactory. Why? Doc talked with me only without doing physical examination (chest auscultation) which is very important! My chief complaint was fever, headache, cough & shortness of breath (D/D: pneumonia/covid/Influenza)

I'm cardiologist, associate professor, practicing back home. I was really surprised to see the condition of an ER physician!

Now will talk about the nurses?
After finishing my chest x ray and blood work &  After seen by the ER doc another nurse suddenly entered into my cubicle & advised me to go to the x ray room for  chest x-ray! When I said "it's done & my doc already discussed about it, then she kept herself silent!"

Another nurse kept me waiting for long long time to give me the appointment for ENT clinic why? ER doc failed to remove the cotton (dislodged from cotyon bud) from my right ear!

When I talked with the reception the ER doc gave me that Ent appointment which was ready before. That thing could be finished early!

Government should recruit good quality of doctors & nurses for better services otherwise people will suffer a lot.

Needs improvement in these sectors.

I took my daughter to Michael Garron Hospital today for a pediatric emergency visit and the experience set a new standard.

From intake to registration, triage, the doctor consult, imaging, nursing care, and transport to and from X-ray, every step felt calm, respectful, and kind. Each staff member showed warmth and professionalism. Communication stayed clear throughout the visit.

This visit was pre-booked online for pediatric emergency imaging. The appointment time was 3:30. Arrival was 30 minutes early as requested. By 4:00, we were back in the car heading home.

The resident took time to explain the imaging results and the treatment plan in plain language. Transitions between teams felt seamless. No confusion. No unnecessary waiting.

The pediatric emergency team delivered an outstanding experience for both child and parent. Gratitude for care delivered with efficiency, humanity, and genuine warmth.

Men's Washroom (accessible) opposite Pharmasave, main floor.
In the photo is the handicapped stall note the metal plate on the floor which catches small wheels on a wheelchair.  The Vertical grab bar is just visible (far right side of toilet), is not reachable with the right hand, when attempting to stand from a wheelchair.  There is no other horizontal bar on the left hand side to assist.  Difficult to either sit or Stand
The picture showing the sinks has the soap at one end, the sinks in the middle and the towel dispenser at the other end.   Proper hand washing requires water, then soap, then rinse off, then dry.  The layout does not support read ready flow for this activity.   Towels not readily reachable from a wheelchair, due to bin size.

D.Gazala and his team were fantastic with explaining, discussing anr performing my Hiatal Hernia surgery. Every part of my recovery has gone precisely as I was told it would. The PSW’s helped in so many was and the nursing staff was second to none, especially Mark he always maintained a high level of care while being professional and informative. Thank’s to everyone that made this go smoothly regards
Don Thurston

name: Centenary Hospital (Scarborough Health Network)
description:
ATTENTION VISITORS:
North Entrance’s long term parking lot is closed. One needs to go to the back of the hospital to access more parking spaces. Long Term rates are cheaper than Short Term Parking rates.
Note: North Entrance’s Short Term parking is open. Mostly disabled parking spaces.

CAUTION:
Went to the wrong hospital for an appointment tonight. I called days before to confirm which hospital it will be, but was given the wrong information.

—->>> The automated text hospital appointment reminder is awesome, but please state the address of the hospital within that message. There’s lots of hospitals in Toronto with the name GENERAL. I don’t want this ever to happen to another family.

Thank you to 2 beautiful Angels. The lady at the Registration Desk helped us by calling the CT Imaging Technician to see if she can accommodate us. Both of these ladies worked together to move our CT Scan appointment from one hospital to another, even though the CT Imaging department was extremely busy tonight.

Thank you so much, you don’t understand how thankful we are. Lugging someone in a wheelchair is not easy. This was an evening appointment too.

Walk In Clinic is near the North Entrance. Turn right as soon as you enter the hospital.

There’s a Shoppers Drugs Mart and a Bubble Tea Shop near the North Entrance.

There’s a LifeLab here, but remember one has to pay for parking.

Short term parking rate is $4 per half hour. Maximum $16. Long term parking rates, please check out my photo.

Floor map and directory is available, just check my pics.

I am very pleased with this hospital in so many ways. First thing, birthing centre is clean, supportive and kind staff, and always willing to help. Dr. C.HUH is amazing as my OB and surgeon during my pregnancy. My daughter coming safely into this world is all thanks to her and I am forever grateful. Including the staff supporting her they all were so supportive and reassuring - the anesthesiologist, the nurses, the support doctor. I am forever ever ever grateful! I spent two nights with so much support after giving birth. Thank you to all the nurses!

Emerg here is also good, staff are welcoming and helpful and doctors are supportive- not too long of a wait either. THANK YOU CENTENARY!

So many negative reviews, its baffling. I have been to this hospital many times, and never once had a negative experience. The doctors are intelligent and well spoken, they tell you things in very easy terms and lay out diagnosis and treatment options in an incredibly easy to understand manner. The nurses are so sweet and kind, some are very funny, while others are very matter of fact and to the point. The support staff work their butts off every single day and do their very best. Complaining about wait times in a hospital is like complaining about mosquitos on a camping trip. Its redundant. In an ideal world, there would be hundreds of doctors and nurses and everyone gets served right away, unfortunately this is the real world. It's currently flu season, COVID season and the city of Toronto has had many cases of RSV and Norovirus. Which ultimately means the emergency rooms will be busier. If you have the flu, or a stomach bug, just go to urgent care, visit your family doctor, or go to a walk in clinic, the hospital in 8/10 Cases isn't going to be able to help you with a virus. The emergency department is treated as a walk in clinic and this factors in majorly to your wait times. Just be realistic, and remember just because someone is a nurse or doctor, doesn't mean they aren't human, and doesn't mean they are incapable of making mistakes. I genuinely thank this hospital and all of its staff, volunteers and community supporters from the bottom of my heart. I was born in this hospital, and over my life thus far they have guided me through countless illness and health scares. they do their best with what they have.

I had hand surgery today at Centenary Hospital, and the experience was excellent.

My hand surgeon, Dr. Javad Sattarian, is a highly skilled and professional surgeon in Toronto. I am truly grateful for his care and expertise. My anesthesiologist, Dr. Mojgan Davalou, was also very kind and reassuring.

The recovery and reception staff in the day surgery unit were wonderful—professional, attentive, and compassionate throughout the entire process.

Thank you to the whole team for taking such good care of me.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Exceptional Labour & Delivery Care

I am extremely grateful for the outstanding care I received during my labour and delivery on October 22-23 ,2025
My  labour and delivery nurse, Rebecca Drobig, stayed with me throughout the entire night shift without taking a break. From the moment she started her shift, she performed a thorough assessment and created a calm, positive, and confident environment. As a first-time mother, her reassurance, professionalism, and continuous support helped me remain relaxed and deliver my baby boy happily and without fear or tension. Her dedication and compassion truly made a huge difference in my birth experience.

I would also like to sincerely thank my consultant, Dr.Usha Harikumar, for her expert guidance, calm reassurance, and constant support throughout my pregnancy and delivery.

My heartfelt appreciation also goes to Dr. Hew, who was present during my labour and provided timely, professional care that contributed to a smooth and positive delivery.

I feel truly blessed to have been supported by such an incredible and compassionate healthcare team while welcoming my first baby into the world. Thank you for making this experience so memorable.

name: St. Joseph’s Health Centre
description:
🌟 My Heartfelt Thanks to St. Joseph’s Health Centre 🌟
On December 26, 2025, I suffered a serious fall that left me with a fractured hip and hand. I was in the hospital until January 23, 2026 — and in that time, the team at St. Joseph’s Health Centre changed my life.
From Emergency to the 4th‑floor Reactivation Unit, every nurse, doctor, therapist, and staff member treated me with dignity, kindness, and respect. Special thanks to Dr. Vincent, Dr. Gutman, Helen from Patient Relations, and the incredible PT/OT teams. You made my hardest days easier.
I even made new friends — including Andy, my neighbor — and shared laughs, stories, and even Star Trek moments with staff. “Live long and prosper” became our little motto.
My experience reminded me how lucky we are to live in Canada, where care is given to everyone, regardless of background, culture, or religion.
To all levels of government and to generous donors:
Please continue supporting our healthcare system.
One day, any of us may need the same care I received.
Thank you, St. Joseph’s. You are truly a place of healing, hope, and humanity.
— Mahbub Hussain

My family and I have needed to come to this hospital so many times throughout my life and each time was a bit different but the overall experience has been similar.

The wait times at this hospital are extremely long even when you have an appointment with a specific department unless it’s for imaging which usually is the fastest experience you can have at this hospital.

The area isn’t great so you get a lot of patients that are not mentally healthy and it creates a stressful experience for others visiting as some of those patients cause an unhealthy environment within the hospital.

The building itself looks like it’s going to need some repairs as there’s some areas where you see visible signs of deterioration in the infrastructure.

With that all being said, I’ve also had some positive experiences with this hospital.

When my mom had her heart attack her initial treatment was at a different hospital and then she was sent over to St. Joseph’s. Her team during the stay was fantastic and attentive and kept her in high spirits.

My mom also had most of my siblings at this hospital and each time the team was wonderful and my mom never wanted to go to another hospital to give birth. My sister in law and a few of my friends have also decided to have their babies here and all of them said it was such a positive experience.

I also have needed to come here for an abscess removal when I was a teenager and it was in a delicate area. They could have kept me awake but offered to put me to sleep for the procedure which I opted in for. The team took great care of me and gave my parents and I aftercare instructions which were easy to follow. I had to go back to have a small tube removed about a month later and it was a seamless experience.

Overall, the staff are mostly fantastic but they are overworked and short staffed. Some upgrades need to be performed on the building and some patients make the experience unpleasant for others but the care team have always done the best they could with the resources available.

I would give 0 stars if I could. I had a horrendous experience. I arrived by ambulance at St Joseph’s emergency at 2 am on Saturday night (the morning of Sep 7).  Paramedics were outstanding.

I was put on a stretcher immediately in a hallway directly under a light and that is where I remained until Tuesday afternoon. Section B blue zone.

I had severe hip and back pain and I could not walk. I had a fever, constant headache exacerbated by the light, and my blood pressure got very low. I was parked directly under a bright light that was on 24/7 in a hallway with constant activity and noise.

The bathroom beside bed BS-05 had urine and blood on the floor, and feces on the toilet seat for almost 24 hours. I reported this multiple times to no avail. That bathroom was filthy.

Although there were a couple of gems (shoutout to the night shift and head nurse on Sunday who made sure I got food, vitals checked and meds when my nurse didn’t), the nursing care was the worst I’ve ever come across. I did not receive 4 meals in a row, all day Sunday and breakfast on Monday even though at each meal I was assured I was added to the list. That nurse who cared for me on Sunday put me at great risk, ignoring all of my needs including food. (She did bring me a dinner tray Sunday night after I didn’t get dinner when everyone else did, only after I made a big fuss, and then I had to ask for it multiple times. She kept saying she was dealing with more urgent situations which I did not see).

I had to beg for pain meds from most nurses and to get any care at all which was completely demeaning. I am not nor was I ever a drug addict.

Getting eye rolls and loud sighs when asking for help was the norm. Only when I was finally well enough on Tuesday  morning to tell the doctor that I either had to get a room or be moved to another hospital, and that I knew people in high places that could report this situation, did I receive any care or respect.

My IV fell out three times. I’m covered in bruises and they were so rough with the needles. No one offered me a walker to get to the bathroom until I had a room on Tuesday afternoon. It was horribly painful getting to the bathroom and back, so I drank as little as possible.

I hope that something is done about the management of this hospital as it goes beyond poor health care, which also clearly remains a huge problem. No one should be in a hallway for 72 hours. The management there is allowing people to work there who should not be allowed to care for people and clearly don’t care, and the filth was shocking.

Unfortunately I was far too sick to take pics of most of what I saw, but I have a few. Something must be done about the state of our health care.

I recently had a muscle biopsy surgery at St. Joseph’s Hospital, and the entire experience was excellent. The nurses, doctors, and staff were extremely professional, caring, and supportive from start to finish. They explained everything clearly, made sure I was comfortable, and treated me with real kindness. I truly appreciate the high level of care I received. Thank you to the whole team for making a stressful procedure as smooth as possible.

I just want to give a shout out for EMS and St.Joesephs ER.  I was brought in via ambulance on Tues Dec 2 and I cannot say enough about how awesome the EMS persons were.  I felt very comfortable with all 3 that were here. From the time of my arrival in emerge, until the time I left, I was very confident that I was being cared for in the best way.  It was extremely busy when I came in and I knew it would be a long wait but I clearly understood that.  In no time at all I had been triaged and off to my section where I saw a Dr in a relatively short time.  I had fluids, electrolytes and nausea meds in an  iv very quickly as well.   I needed a chest x-ray and the gentleman that did it was the most kind person.   Someone else came in behind me, she was an elderly women and he asked her (as he did me) if she was ok, is there anything you need, she said her family doesn't know she's there.   He said you didn't call them.. and she said no, I don't have a phone.  He went and got his phone, asked her for her son's number and he let her talk to her son to let him now.  It was heartwarming to watch.  So, from the time my son called an ambulance till the time I left, I have 0 complaints,  Both EMS and emerge staff were dealing with patients who were rude, had mental health issues, and not once did I hear a complaint.   Thank you to all those involved, considering how horrible I felt and how frightened I was, you made my experience so much better than I would have thought it would be.  You are underappreciated and I'd just like you to know that I felt very well cared for and appreciate you....every one of you.

Susan

name: St. Michael’s Hospital
description:
Their Diabetes Clinic is excellent. Otherwise, my experience in Ultrasound, Nephrology, and Urology is that they do not respect patients. We are human, not objects. During 2 different procedures on the same day, one while mostly unclothed, other people walked in and out of the room. One came in and left the door open as I was fully exposed trying to get off the gurney. In addition, some staff are very judgmental about our personal problems. Others are rude and unhelpful, not even bothered to write complete sentences in emails or provide contact info with referrals. They don't even say hello or make eye contact when taking blood pressure!

The emergency department is hit or miss. I had a cyst removed and they were very nice, but did not do the follow-up to arrange home care. I called back as I had a hole in my back stuffed with gauze that hadn't been changed in 5 days, so was told to come back. Then, even after repeating this info, the triage nurse gave me serious attitude for coming there and not a walk-in clinic. I was told to come there!

Their Patient Relations did an "investigation." They couldn't figure out who was walking in and out, meaning the doctor and nurse did not know either. Who were these strange people entering during medical procedures? But the managers will remind them, because sometimes the staff forget about privacy. That should be instinctive. I get comprehensive and deliberate privacy from RMTs, so hospital staff can do the same.

Finally, on the way out, they have the nerve to have young people accost you for donations! They should have senior staff stand there so they can have real discussions, because the lack of respect is systemic despite their many signs telling patients to be respectful.

Whatever happens to me in the future does not matter. What truly matters is that, five days after my open-heart surgery, I can say with full confidence that I was cared for by some of the coolest, most skilled surgeons and the kindest, most compassionate nurses on earth.

This hospital is truly iconic. The level of professionalism, humanity, and dedication I experienced here goes far beyond expectations. You should be immensely proud—this hospital stands as a true Canadian symbol of care, compassion, and help.

I will always be grateful for the treatment and respect I received here.

I really wish I could put zero stars and not give this hospital a single star. However, I am writing this as the daughter of a disabled patient, Daniel Cubias, who was recently subjected to appalling treatment at St. Michael's Hospital.

My father was physically assaulted by security personnel while in a vulnerable state. Despite being a patient under the hospital's care, he was forcibly pulled out of a bathroom after only five minutes based on a baseless and discriminatory assumption of drug use. To make matters worse, security used physical force against him simply for being loud on a phone call—a noise issue that should have been handled with de-escalation, not assault.

Treating a disabled person with such aggression and denying them basic privacy is a violation of the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Patient Bill of Rights. St. Michael's is supposed to be a place of healing, but for my father, it was a place of trauma and physical harm.

We will be filing a formal complaints with Patient Relations and the Patient Ombudsman, and we will be pursuing this further. If you have a loved one with a disability, be incredibly careful. The security culture here is militaristic and dangerous.

☆ My Birthplace (September 1987)

✅️ Enormous facility

✅️ Hours Of Operation: 24 hours, 7 days

✅️ Heart of Downtown Toronto

✅️ Close to T.T.C bus stops

✅️ Washrooms are available and accessible 🚻

✅️ Parking for staff in garage

✅️ Food court for patients, visitors and staff

✅️ Seems really clean

I delivered my baby via c‑section at St. Michael’s and had the most incredible, positive experience from start to finish. Every single person I encountered, from the triage nurse to the L&D team, including Ashley and Teresa, as well as the medical staff — showed such genuine compassion, patience, and kindness. They created an environment where I felt supported, respected, and truly cared for.
I was in labour for nearly 44 hours, originally planning for a vaginal delivery before ultimately needing an emergency c‑section. Throughout that long and emotional journey, the team made sure I was as comfortable as possible and never left me feeling uncertain or alone. They kept me informed at every stage, honoured my birth plan wherever they could, and approached every decision with empathy and professionalism.
The anesthesiologist and OBGYN were exceptional — calm, reassuring, and thorough in explaining each step, which made a world of difference during such a vulnerable moment.
I’m deeply grateful to the entire team for their dedication and the outstanding care they provided. You are all so appreciated, more than words can express.

name: Sunnybrook Hospital
description:
If I could give zero stars, I absolutely would.

My family’s experience with the Maxillofacial/Dentistry department at Sunnybrook was devastating.

For months, my mother was seen repeatedly by this department and the Emergency Department while suffering from escalating and unbearable pain. She returned again and again because nothing that was prescribed, including multiple rounds of antibiotics and other medications nothing was helping.

Despite this, her condition was repeatedly dismissed and attributed to a “dead bone” or infection. In fact, the team was preparing to perform surgery for what they believed was a completely different issue, while the actual cause  cancer  went undiagnosed.

The level of dismissal we experienced was shocking. At one point, a nurse even contacted me at my workplace and told me that my mother’s situation “was not dangerous,” that they had “seen much worse,” and that she should stop coming to the hospital. This was said while my mother was clearly suffering and desperately trying to get help for severe, ongoing pain.

What makes this even more alarming is that my mother was later diagnosed elsewhere with stage 4 cancer, including an aggressive tumor measuring nearly 7 cm, in the exact area that had been repeatedly assessed at Sunnybrook.

When another maxillofacial clinic examined her, they immediately recognized that something was seriously wrong and escalated her care right away. The contrast between that clinic’s urgency and the months of dismissal we experienced at Sunnybrook was staggering.

Equally upsetting was the lack of empathy shown by the doctors we encountered. My mother was in extreme pain and distress, yet she was treated with very little compassion. Instead of feeling supported, she often felt dismissed and made to feel as though her suffering did not matter.

There was also very little continuity of care. She rarely saw the same doctor twice, and no one appeared to take ownership of her case or investigate why her symptoms were worsening despite repeated treatments failing.

After everything that happened, I raised these concerns with Sunnybrook’s Patient Experience office and repeatedly asked for a proper review. Despite the seriousness of the situation, there has been no real accountability taken for what occurred. Responses were delayed and missed unless I reached out again, complete lack of urgency and lack of accountability is extremely concerning and disappointing.

Hospitals should be places where patients are listened to and taken seriously. Unfortunately, our experience with the Sunnybrook Maxillofacial department was the opposite. The delay in recognizing a serious condition, combined with the lack of empathy and lack of accountability afterward, has been incredibly difficult for our family.

I hope that by sharing this experience, improvements will be made so that other patients do not have to endure what my mother went through.

Dear Sunnybrook Staff,

I wanted to take a moment to thank all of the staff for your dedication, compassion, and support during this incredibly difficult time for our family.

In particular, I would like to express my heartfelt appreciation to Sonia and Beverly for consistently going above and beyond. From stepping away from your primary location to provide testing in a more convenient setting, to offering helpful tips that made our days run more smoothly, and always greeting us with a warm smile — your kindness did not go unnoticed and meant more to us than you may realize.

Thank you, Carmen, for making Kelly’s stay as comfortable as possible, especially on those long days during chemo. You truly mastered the positioning of those pillows every single time, and that level of care made such a difference.

I would also like to thank Sophie at reception for always providing words of encouragement, which were truly appreciated during stressful moments. A special thank you as well to Ingrid for taking the time to ensure we fully understood every part of the treatment process, and for patiently helping my mom navigate Kelly’s wheelchair throughout the hospital with such care and compassion.
All of your professionalism, empathy, and genuine care helped make a very challenging experience far more manageable for our family. We are deeply grateful.

With sincere appreciation

My experience at Sunnybrook Health Sciences Centre was astonishingly disappointing for a facility that prides itself on excellence.

The nursing staff appeared far more invested in their screens surfing the internet, and getting the latest gossip than in the human beings under their care. It was difficult not to notice the amount of time spent disengaged while basic patient needs went unattended.  Attentiveness felt inconsistent at best, and responses to requests sometimes came across as inconveniences rather than professional responsibilities. From our perspective, it felt as though patient care was not the clear priority it should have been.

As for the physicians, the attitude was equally troubling. Questions were met with thinly veiled impatience, as though seeking clarity about one’s own medical condition were an unreasonable demand. Communication was minimal, updates were sparse, and any attempt at engagement felt like interrupting something far more important. The air of superiority was unmistakable.

For an institution of this reputation, one would expect professionalism, compassion, and accountability. Instead, the experience felt impersonal, dismissive, and unnecessarily arrogant. Patients deserve care — not condescension.

Our family would like to sincerely thank Sunnybrook Holland Centre for the care my mother received during her revision hip surgery.
We are especially grateful to Dr.
Pincus and his team.
My mother is elderly and we were very worried, but Dr. Pincus arranged the surgery quickly, which reduced the time she had to remain in bed and lowered the risk of complications.
We would also like to thank Maria and the entire team for their patience and support. Their kindness and coordination made everything much smoother for our family.

Thanks also to the physiotherapists who helped my mother regain mobility step by step with encouragement and care.
Everyone we met treated my mother with kindness and respect, and we truly appreciate all that was done for her.
Your dedication, skill, and compassion made a real difference in her life-and in ours.

I had my child here; unfortunately, Dr. Richard is the only good doctor here. He is very respectful and capable and listens to patients, in my experience. The nurses and the hospital only care about money; they don't listen, are tyrants, and there are many students who don't know what to do. Asked not to have any students; they didn't respect that. Asked not to cut the umbilical cord right away; they did. The head nurse was very rude and treated you like trash, especially if you are a man. The staff and protocols in the birthing section were awful. Greedy and inhuman as it gets.

name: Achieva Health
description:
The receptionist is so welcoming and kind—always has a smile on her face :) The physiotherapist my mom worked with was very knowledgeable and patient. My mom completed 6 sessions and began feeling a little better. Also learned lots of ways to manage her osteoarthritis pain. Grateful for the help!

Ekta is fantastic! My husband and I go together; me for my pinched neck nerve and his PMR hands. Hubby went to a private clinic after his knee surgery and even said he got better service here. I almost wish I had other ailments to keep going back, but thank goodness I don't :)

My Husband and I have been coming to this location at 355 Eglinton ave. The Physiotherapist here Ms Ekta Bhavnani, and Shiraz are amazing people. Very courteous and friendly and helpful. And have excellent knowledge of their work.The receptionist Ms Navdeep is a very accommodating and always cheerful and helping, especially when we have to reschedule our appointments due to doctors visits. This is very welcoming place for those seeking physio treatment.

Don’t make any appointments because they don’t have parking space,because of finding parking,when you late ,they mark at no show

If you’re recovering from a serious injury or surgery, I strongly recommend avoiding this clinic. I came here for OHIP-covered physiotherapy after surgery and had a very disappointing experience.

The sessions are supposed to be around 15 minutes, but I received less than 5 minutes of actual treatment.
While Ekta was polite, she showed very little accountability or concern for my recovery. Her knowledge of elbow rehabilitation seemed limited, and she made no effort to tailor the treatment to my needs.

Every session felt rushed, and she kept asking if it was my last one—even though my condition clearly required more support. At another OHIP-covered clinic, I received proper, consistent care for six months. The contrast was night and day.

This clinic does not provide the level of professionalism or care that recovery from a serious injury demands. I would not recommend it to anyone in need of proper physiotherapy.

name: Community Physiotherapy Clinic
description:
My mom just had the worst experience ever. She has been coming to this clinic before, her English is not that well so anytime there was any documentation that needed signature they would allow her to bring it home for one of us to help her with the translation.

Well, last week she was not only refused to take the questionnaire home, but she was almost forced to sign it on the spot with the receptionist saying “or call your son and get him to help you over the phone”. She was so rude to the point that my mom started crying.

When I came to the clinic, she would not even let me look at the document myself, she would take the paper away from my hands every time I tried to read the back. Her lack of professionalism and empathy had made my mom decide to go somewhere else despite the amazing nurse.

The staff working there is nice but, the Main therapist lady is extremely RUDE, she thinks ppl are there to ask her for a favour. She talks to clients on a rude and abrupt manner.
A terrible experience.

Van is experienced, and very empathetic to your situation. Very encouraging and supportive and gives you hope for healing.

Very unprofessional. Don’t know how to process with the insurance.

The staff are kind and understanding, especially towards elderly and those with language barriers

name: High Park Physiotherapy & Sports Medicine Clinic
description:
I am obsessed, blessed and eternally impressed and amazed by this place. I'm truly so lucky. Every single therapist here truly cares, and by that actually are channeling real medicine.

The good news first. Their suggested exercises work very well. IF you are strictly an OHIP patient, and have no other options, then grit your teeth and put up with the chaos, endless waiting, and rudeness of Pearce, who is a man that doesn't work well under stress. These days there is a lot of that in the medical field. Anyone can tell you that. Their intentions are good, you are given exercises based on an in person interview with a physiotherapist, and then an ASSISTANT gives you a poorly photocopied sheet of exercises. As part of the treatment plan the assistant physio checks you, and usually 2, 3, up to 4 other people, at the same time, going back and forth between all of you when your scheduled therapy is taking place.

IF you have private health insurance, go elsewhere. IF not, and all that is important is that you get better, no matter the method, then this may be your best option.

Dear High Park Physiotherapy,

I am writing to document and share my concerns regarding my recent experience at your clinic.

I first called on a Tuesday to book an appointment. I was asked if I could come in later that same day; however, I was unavailable and instead accepted an appointment for the following day at 1:30 p.m. I was very pleased to have been booked so quickly.

When booking, I explained to the receptionist that my family doctor referred me to High Park Physiotherapy specifically because your clinic accepts patients covered under Ontario Health Insurance. The receptionist informed me that OHIP-covered clients may have longer wait times compared to those with private insurance, and asked if I had private insurance. I confirmed that I do, through Desjardins, and she was pleased to hear this and confirmed my appointment.

I also explained that all services I receive should be billed under my Desjardins coverage, and that anything not covered could be billed through OHIP. I told her I would arrive early to provide my insurance information since she preferred not to collect it over the phone.

The next day, I arrived one hour early to give staff time to process my coverage. However, an hour later, another receptionist asked me to sign a document stating that I was required to pay $150 for the initial assessment, with reimbursement to come directly from my insurance company.

I explained that I had already confirmed with the previous receptionist that your clinic accepts direct billing, and that I had come early specifically so the coverage could be verified. At that point, I phoned Desjardins myself. A representative spoke directly with your receptionist and confirmed that I am covered 100% for physiotherapy. Despite this, I was still told I must pay upfront.

The receptionist even called Desjardins again, and they repeated the same confirmation: I am covered at 100%. Still, the clinic refused to honor the direct billing and would not proceed with my appointment unless I paid the $150 directly.

Shortly after, the Director introduced himself and explained that High Park Physiotherapy does not accept direct billing from Desjardins because the insurer has not reliably paid invoices in the past. While I understand the frustration this may cause a business, the repeated insistence that I pay out of pocket—despite my insurer confirming coverage multiple times—was both confusing and concerning.

In total, I was told no fewer than nine times that I had to pay directly, even after Desjardins had clearly confirmed otherwise. Unfortunately, I was unable to proceed with my appointment as a result.

I am disappointed by this experience and would like clarification from your office:

Why does High Park Physiotherapy not honor Desjardins’ direct billing despite the insurer’s confirmation of coverage?

Why was I not informed of this policy when I first booked my appointment?

Dear Pearse,

I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to you and your entire team at Bloor West Physiotherapy and High Park Physiotherapy for the exceptional care and support you’ve provided to my mother.

Your kindness, professionalism, and attentiveness to her needs have played a significant role in her recovery. From the very beginning, we felt genuinely cared for — and seeing such positive progress in her condition has brought us both great comfort and joy.

We would also like to shine a light on your clinic and the truly diligent and authentic work you do. Through the work of our nonprofit organization Yurta Mira, we can help your clinic receive an official award or certificate in recognition of your contribution to the health and well-being of your patients.

Please extend my thanks to everyone involved in her treatment. It means a great deal to both of us.

We look forward to continuing her care with your team and feel truly fortunate to be in such capable hands.

Warm regards,
Afo G.

Why did I wait so long to seek relief from my back pain? High Park Physiotherapy was recommended by a friend who was treated successfully there for pain caused by a fall, and kept telling me that  I should go there because I was experiencing pain for a long time and only had some relief from prescribed pain medication which was only masking the pain. During my first visit to High Park Physiotherapy I was examined thoroughly , and a  treatment plan was prescribed, firstly to determine the root of the problem, treat it,  followed by an excercise program.
I am happy to say that after three sessions, I started to feel less pain and regain moblity that was less painful.
I cannot say enough about how the staff at the clinic was professional and very friendly.
I would like to add that Dhruvi was very accommodating when it came to changing my apointment time schedule and say a special thank you to my physiotherapist Prya, for helping me with the excercise program, in taking extra time to explain the benefits of each excercise, and to help me to remember them for repeating them at home.
With the positive results I gained, I highly recommend this clinic.

name: Kings Professional Physiotherapy Clinic
description:
Beware.  If these people put as much efforts in their actual treatment as they do in trying to squeeze every dollar out of your benefits, they would the best in the GTA. Never again.

I've been attending this clinic for many, many years.  The service from front door to physio team is professional and exceptional.  I would recommend this clinic ...100?%.

Outstanding Service and Professional Care!
I had an amazing experience at KingsPhysio. From the moment I walked in, the staff were warm, welcoming, and incredibly knowledgeable. The physiotherapists took the time to understand my concerns, provided a clear treatment plan, and helped me recover faster than I expected. I highly recommend KingsPhysio to anyone looking for expert care and genuine support on their road to recovery!

Very helpful staff and would definitely recommend this clinic for therapy.

I've been coming to Kings since 2023, four times before and currently the fifth time. They're experts at making my lumbar back and neck pains go away. I'm 78 and on my own so I appreciate their friendly, relaxed approach to my physical problems. I was referred here by my Family Physician. Five stars!

name: Kingsway Physiotherapy Centre
description:
I was visiting my family doctor in the same building and decided to stop by Pinpoint Health Kingsway to inquire about their services. Abi
was very helpful and welcoming. She even called my insurance company to check my coverage for me, which I really appreciated. They also offer free consultations, which is great. I’m very satisfied with the experience and the professionalism of the clinic. I’ll definitely be booking an appointment with them soon!

I had an exceptional experience at this health clinic from start to finish. The reception staff is warm, welcoming, and incredibly organized. They handled all of my insurance details directly and made the entire process completely hassle‑free.

My appointment with the doctor was equally impressive. Rather than rushing through the visit, they took the time to thoroughly understand my existing condition, review my history, and ask thoughtful questions before offering any guidance. I truly felt heard and cared for.

This location specifically had convenient parking at street level with additional paid parking lots directly beside the clinic.

The clinic in general combines great service, knowledgeable staff, and a high level of care all in one place. I highly recommend them to anyone looking for healthcare services within PinPoint Health's area of expertise, which is quite vast.

I had an excellent experience at PinPoint Health Kingsway- Physiotherapy & Massage Therapy Centre. Zawar is an outstanding physiotherapist who truly cares about his patients’ recovery and well-being.

From my very first appointment, Zawar took the time to thoroughly assess my condition and answer all of my questions.

Thanks to his dedication and expertise in acupuncture, I’ve seen significant improvement and feel much stronger.

I’ve had such a great experience at Pinpoint Kingsway! I initially saw Dr. Alyssa for naturopathy. She was incredibly knowledgeable, took her time explaining everything in detail, and made sure I fully understood all the post-treatment care instructions. The clinic itself is very clean and welcoming, and the staff are always kind and professional.

Recently, I was referred to Zawar for physiotherapy, and I’m so glad I went. He really took his time to assess and explain what was going on, and I genuinely felt instant relief after my first visit. You can tell he truly cares about his patients’ recovery.

I highly recommend this clinic for anyone looking for naturopathic care or physiotherapy!

Zawar is Awesome! SO knowledgeable and trained in medical acupuncture. The pain in my shoulder is improving already! Go see him! You won’t be disappointed!
Abi is a lovely receptionist with a friendly and welcoming demeanour! What a team!

name: LifeMark Physiotherapy – Toronto Bathurst
description:
Special Thanks to Edward Xiao (PT) for the help as of today January 28, 2026.

I was having severe/agonizing pain due to my left tennis elbow injury.
From October 2025 to December 2025, I've been struggling with the pain injury and having difficulty sleeping then also it affects my work activities and family responsibilities. During this timeframe, I was assuming that pain will be relieved by taking medication and basic massages. But it's not.

I'm the first week of January 2026, I started physiotherapy at Lifemark (Bathurst and St. Clair) with Edward.
Within (1) one month the severity of my left tennis elbow pain injury has been significantly reduced and improved.

A strategic and planned daily exercises, one a week specific muscle acupuncture, muscle ultrasound and electrical muscle stimulation really helps in improving my left tennis elbow injury.

Tennis elbow injury is a long term injury that requires long term treatment. But with the help of Edward, the pain and muscle stiffness were significantly improved on the first month of therapy.  Regular daily exercises should help the muscle injury improve in the next few weeks.

Not having had physiotherapy in my adult life, I was pleasantly surprised and relieved by the knowledge and assistance of staff, both physio Hillary and assistant Kane in treating my strained shoulder.  I did not understand what was wrong with it and was fearful of disability but in one 20-minute session I found new healing and hope.  After four sessions the situation had greatly improved and I have a good idea of how to continue to keep my shoulder healthy and strong.  Thank you.

Had a car accident where I had a concoction and bad whiplash. They were great helping me get through the concoction. Terry is helping me through my left hand still going numb. Sonia is the best acupuncturist I ever had. Hits the points bang on. Chris the message therapist loves to hit those nots. very strong hands  Love this place. Great people all around.

Lifemark physio is a great rehab centre, Terry for physio and Sonya for acupuncture have helped my arm and wrist pain tremendously, trying different methods and giving me helpful stretches that will help me recover and continue to heal when weekly physio isn't as necessary. The reception staff like Jessica and the scheduling is always great and they usually give me a paper copy of upcoming appointments or I can see them online with vac rehab portal. Thanks for all the great work you all do to help others feel better!!

Raluca Alb, a physiotherapist at the Lifemark clinic at Bathurst & St. Clair worked with me to resolve my disabling back pain. I was taught how to do a series of stretches and strengthening exercises (in just 2 sessions with her) that has corrected my issue and will prevent it from reoccurring - if I continue to do these exercises at home. Raluca explained to me very clearly the physiology involved and with charm and wit instilled the motivation in me to maintain a daily practice to avoid this issue in my future. I feel very fortunate that this clinic is in my neighbourhood and staffed with an excellent physiotherapist. Thank you Raluca.

name: O’Connor Rehabilitation & Wellness Clinic
description:
This is very frustrating. I have been trying to book an appointment for three days straight, and no one ever answers the phone. I’m surprised that all calls seem to go unanswered.

As this was my first ever full body massage, I was not sure what to expect. From the moment I got through the front door, I was welcomed by Candace in a very professional and friendly manner. From the filling out of the forms to introducing me to Sandra, my Massage Therapist. I found the treatment itself to be both relaxing and refreshing & Sandra to be very professional asking if she was applying too much or too little force or pressure, all the time explaining what she was going to do as she was doing it. Overall, a very enjoyable 1-hour experience, I have already booked my next Massage Therapy.

My first visit yesterday and I was very impressed. Nice comfy clinic space and great staff.
Jordan did my intake and had a great clinical manner. He really knows his stuff and made me feel very confident in the care I would be receiving. Cheryl on the front desk was a pleasure to deal with.
Looking forward to my series of treatments and resolving the pain and dysfunction I have been dealing with. Super happy I found these folks.

What a dump , the female staff member pretty much just stares at the tv , mumbles very few words (if you can understand her).
This is a cash register nothing more , the equipment is dirty ,muscle stimulator  barely functioning, the ministry needs to be aware of what is going on here , I have never seen staff so utterly disengaged from the clients in my life , the good reviews astound me , obviously most people have not been to a properly run professional clinic .
You are pretty much on your own here , the female staff member may have uttered or more correctly mumbled a total of twenty words over a ten week period , thrust equipment at me and gave me blank stares , you are expected to run your own program here , don’t bug the staff especially the female , she’s got tv to watch, I will be contacting the ministry, dump ,pure dump !!!!
George Condie

The staff are not professional especially recipients. She acting very rude. They changed the appointment by them self and the did not inform me about the changes. When I come to the appointment, they said you don’t have an appointment at this time. So I  asked her for rescheduling the appointment for my convenient time but She was not answering  for next appointment. She acting like the physician in this world.

name: Physical Therapy Services
description:
Highly recommended! Extremely helpful and understanding Physiotherapy providers.
Very knowledgable Physio and assistant who explain everything patiently, as well as providing very good treatments and exercises.
They also do their best to accomodate their clients with as short of a waitlist as possible compared to other physio clinics.
Thank you!

Clinic is super accommodating and the staff is really kind! Treatment is affordable and really good. They also helped me get access to supportive devices.

This clinic provides top-notch services, quick fast and efficient, and very person oriented services very kind people
Thank you and very lovey staff as well

I had problems with my shoulder and my family doctor referred me here because it's covered by OHIP.  All I can say is that I achieved my goal in getting better, thanks to the professional work that I've received from this clinic.
I read some reviews about the place needing some fixing, but, this is beyond the owner's responsibility. Please remember that this is not a private clinic and it's not located in a fancy location.
Thank you Sam. You're kind.

My experience at this physiotherapy clinic is going purse.Go people , the work with and they care for what you eat , they do , and their very , very , very experiencing what they do i consider them no 1 people should come here physio for seniors and ohip

name: Physio Wellness (Jane)
description:
Well where do I begin? I started going to this location for physio treatments in late June of last year. From the first visit I was made to feel comfortable and important! I wasn't treated like just another patient or number. The physiotherapist Sneha is just wonderful! She listens to your concerns, she is compassionate and goes above and beyond to ensure you are being cared for to the best of her ability! She is very personable as well. All the staff from reception to the owners are all friendly and genuine.  They all take a personal interest in you for your well being. The physio assistants are wonderful and caring! They know their stuff! I am still a patient due to the nature of my health issues and I am very pleased and glad that I chose this location 909 Jane street. My recommendation is if you have need for physio treatments come here you won't regret it! Also they have a chiropractor Dr. Arvin on staff he is great!

My son has been attending physio wellness and all the staff are wonderful. It is a really friendly environment and great location, convenient.
This is a great place because every time he receives treatment he feels better. I would definitely recommend it to anyone in need. I have been extremely happy and not to mention they have the best physio therapists too.

Just want to share how happy I am with the services at Physio Wellness.  I've taken my Dad for 6 weeks and I'm very pleased to say he's doing GREAT 👍.  On our day one visit we met with Sneha- Physiotherapist and she told us exactly what to do. My Dad followed the instructions and today I can say it does work. Sneha is so Compassionate and very caring. I can tell she's very Passionate about her job. Ha - Administrator is so PLEASANT to deal with. Just love her Aloe Vera Plant and I'm looking forward to get my own plant soon🙏. Tasha R.- Receptionist very calm and understanding,  however, she owes us for always taking our Temperature prior to go to the treatment room 😀. To All the other Staff we appreciate your services and continue to do what you do Best!. Thanks again and all the best for the future. Great Service 👍👍👍👍👍👍

Physiotherapy wellness is one of the best place ever to get treatment.  Friendly faces and and very good mannerisms. I am encouraging the entire public to direct your  care right here . My therapist name is Sneha and she  is one of the best,  she does her work very well and by the time she gets through with you,  the pain is gone.
Try them today and you won't regret it.

Thank you all for the beautiful job you did with my back pain. I started going to Physiotherapy wellness a little over three weeks ago, and I must admit the services they provide is beyond excellent. When I first visited Physiotherapy wellness, I could barely walk or bend because of my work mishap. Physiotherapy wellness assured me that that could improve me, and they did what they promised. During the visit, I could move faster and use the stairs. Thanks to Physiotherapy wellness, I am without pain.

Mike C

name: Physiotherapy Associates
description:
I started going to this Clinic over a month and a half ago and I must say that the level of professionalism, courtesy, and care is the Best. Mona is such a great receptionist and so kind and caring. My Physiotherapist, Mr.Ison Abraham, has helped me immensely to overcome the pain that was once in my right shoulder and arm. All in all, a great experience at Physiotherapy Associates. I would recommend it!

Since being diagnosed with back sprain nearly 2 months ago now, I have been receiving physiotherapy treatment from Ison who has done a great job in my recovery process and time and now I have improved a lot.  Highly recommended visiting the clinic!

Mona is the best receptionist ever!!! The staff is so friendly and really listens to your concerns. Highly recommend this place for your physio.

my first visit went great felt at ease and really comfortable and relaxed same with the 2nd visit and the receptionist is a great friendly lady. I would recommend this place to anyone who needs it.

Never heard back from them. :(

name: PT Health – Main and Gerrard Physiotherapy
description:
I can’t say enough good things about PT Physio at Main and Gerard. From the moment you walk in, the space is spotless, calm, and professional — you immediately feel like you’re in good hands.

The entire team is incredibly patient, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in your progress. They take the time to explain everything clearly and guide you through each step, which makes such a difference when you’re rehabbing or working through an injury.

A huge shout-out to Michelle in particular — her patience, encouragement, and thoughtful approach made every session feel supportive and motivating. She truly goes above and beyond.

If you’re looking for a physio clinic with clean facilities, great energy, and practitioners who actually care, this is the place. Highly recommend.

My experience of treatment at Main & Gerrard Physio was excellent. It’s amazing how the simplest of tiny exercises made just a huge difference. Staff is professional, kind and courteous. I watched how they treat all their patients with kindness, care and empathy while explaining benefits, demonstrating correct positions and pushing for best results. A gentle but firm touch.

I would recommend this group for physiotherapy needs.

I had an excellent experience at this physiotherapy clinic. The team is highly professional,  and genuinely cares about patient outcomes. The treatment was very hands-on and tailored specifically to my condition, not a one-size-fits-all approach.

The atmosphere is friendly and welcoming. I felt supported, motivated, and confident throughout my rehabilitation. I would highly recommend this clinic to anyone looking for high-quality physiotherapy.

I had an excellent experience at Main and Gerrard Physiotherapy. I went there with severe back and leg pain, and the entire staff was kind and welcoming.
Hadi was extremely professional, thoughtful, and attentive throughout my treatment. He took the time to understand my condition and created a clear, effective treatment plan with exercises tailored to my needs. Michelle was very supportive, and Neha, the assistant, was also helpful. I truly appreciate their care.
I highly recommend this clinic to anyone dealing with pain or injury.

I injured my left ankle visited this place based on distance from my home and review I read online, I am glad I made a wright choice. The clinic is very clean, well organized. Hadi is very professional, supportive, and Michelle is very cheerful , thoughtful , they did a great job on my recovery. Highly recommend this clinic.

name: PT Health – Melrose Physiotherapy
description:
I had a great experience at Lifemark Physiotherapy. My therapist, Neha, was knowledgeable and supportive throughout my treatment. She helped me regain motion in my neck and left shoulder and significantly reduced the pain in my back. I felt real progress and appreciated how clearly she explained everything. Highly recommend!

I went for a n hour massage. I explained my situation and during the session I also said that she was ignoring my lower back which is why I was there in the first place. I then left feeling worse than before. It took me 3 weeks to recover as the massage put me in a flare with my fibromyalgia. I went in when I was feeling better and talked to the receptionist saying I had a complaint. She was not helpful in the least and rude. Very disappointed

Addendum :
